tool_input.prompt:
You are the **Phase 0 Coordinator** for the Phlix UI Coverage build, running as a Claude Code subagent. You DELEGATE the actual work — you spawn fresh step-level subagents for each role and wait for each synchronously. You do NOT write impl/review/test/docs code inline yourself. Your job this run: drive **step 0.4 ONLY** all the way to `done` (merged PR + STATUS updated + cumulative pass), then RETURN a checkpoint report. Do not start 0.5.

## Authoritative files (READ FIRST, in this order)
1. `/home/sites/phlix/PHLIX_UI_PLAN.md` — the plan (read the Architecture, Runtime & async, and Phase 0 sections at minimum; you may skim the rest).
2. `/home/sites/phlix/PHLIX_UI_STATUS.md` — single source of truth for progress. 0.1/0.2/0.3 are `done`. Your step is 0.4 (currently `todo`).
3. `/home/sites/phlix/steps/0.4-admin-spa-scaffold.md` — the FULL spec for step 0.4. Implement EXACTLY this, no extra scope.
4. Skim `/home/sites/phlix/steps/0.3-swoole-uv-install-ci.worklog.md` to learn how prior steps were actually run + the worklog format.

Create the worklog `/home/sites/phlix/steps/0.4-admin-spa-scaffold.worklog.md` (empty to start). Every role-agent you spawn MUST read the step spec + worklog FIRST and append its results to the worklog before finishing — the worklog + STATUS are the shared memory across fresh subagents.

## Repo
`phlix-server` at `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-server` (clean on `master`). This is a phlix-server-only step (the admin-ui SPA lives under `phlix-server/admin-ui/`, builds to `phlix-server/public/assets/admin/`). Docs go to `phlix-docs` (separate PR if needed, but prefer to keep 0.4's docs in the phlix-server README + a phlix-docs/dev page — ONE repo per PR; if you touch phlix-docs, that's a SEPARATE PR from the phlix-server PR).

## Per-step QA cycle (MANDATORY — run every role; each a FRESH subagent, SYNCHRONOUS, all on the feat/ branch BEFORE the PR merges so impl+fixes+tests+docs land in ONE phlix-server PR):
- **A. Implementer** → spawn subagent_type `php-implementer`. Brief: implement exactly the In-scope items of the 0.4 spec to satisfy its Acceptance criteria; no extra features; reuse the EXISTING JWT/token mechanism in `public/assets/js/api-client.js` (don't invent new auth); wire `/admin` + `/admin/*` dispatch in BOTH `public/index.php` AND `src/Server/Http/HttpHandler.php`; keep existing SSR `/admin/plugins` + `/admin/dashboard` working; decide & DOCUMENT commit-built-assets-vs-build-in-CI (pick one, be consistent — recommend gitignore `admin-ui/node_modules` and commit the built `public/assets/admin/` bundle so the server has no Node build dep at runtime, but you decide and record it). Append a change summary to the worklog. Edits files only — does NOT run git.
- **B. Reviewer** → spawn `code-reviewer`. Review THIS step's diff vs Acceptance criteria + security (XSS/SQLi/path-traversal/auth — esp. the AdminMiddleware gating + the SPA shell route) + async/resident-memory rules + Webman conventions. Numbered findings or `NO FINDINGS` to the worklog. (It may use oac:code-review.)
- **C. Fixer** → spawn `php-implementer` (as Fixer). Fix EVERY numbered finding; record resolutions in the worklog. **⟲ LOOP B⇄C until `NO FINDINGS`.**
- **D. TestEngineer** → spawn `test-engineer`. Build/extend tests for THIS step's changes to ~100% on new/touched code: Vitest (API client incl. auth header + token refresh path + error handling; shell/nav render; admin-guard redirect logic) + a PHP test for AdminAppController (returns the shell; 302 redirect for non-admin). Run the verification commands and paste REAL output + coverage% into the worklog. If RED → back to C (Fixer); ⟲ until GREEN. (May use oac:test-generation, oac:verification-before-completion.)
- **E. Scribe** → spawn `scribe`. Update phlix-docs (`dev/` admin-SPA page: stack, dev server, build step, where it mounts) + phlix-server README (`admin-ui` build commands) + CHANGELOG + in-code docblocks. COMPLETE + accurate. No behavioral code changes. Record what was documented in the worklog. NOTE: phlix-docs changes are a SEPARATE PR from the phlix-server PR (one repo per PR).

## Verification commands (the step is not done until these are GREEN — paste REAL output into the worklog):
```bash
cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server/admin-ui && npm install && npm run build && npm run test
cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && ./vendor/bin/phpunit && ./vendor/bin/phpstan analyze src/ --level=9 && ./vendor/bin/phpcs --standard=PSR12 src/
```

## GIT CYCLE at the END of the step (after the cycle is clean + green):
- phlix-server PR first, then (if docs changed) a separate phlix-docs PR.
- Follow the Cardinal Rules below EXACTLY. **CALIBER PRE-COMMIT-HOOK HAZARD:** committing in the LIVE repos triggers a `caliber refresh` hook that force-`git add`s `.agents/`/`.claude/`/`.caliber/` and on some repos CORRUPTS the git index ("Error building trees"). Do NOT `--no-verify`. Land each commit via a **throwaway clone** (clones carry no hooks):
  ```bash
  # in the live repo on your feat branch with edits applied:
  git add <intended files> 2>/dev/null; git diff --cached > /tmp/0.4-server.patch || git diff > /tmp/0.4-server.patch
  # (capture the FULL intended delta incl. new files — use `git add -N` for new untracked files first so they appear in the diff, OR copy new files manually into the clone)
  unset GITHUB_TOKEN && gh repo clone detain/phlix-server /tmp/phlix-server-0.4
  cd /tmp/phlix-server-0.4 && git checkout -b feat/0-4-admin-spa-scaffold
  # apply the delta (git apply /tmp/0.4-server.patch) AND copy any NEW files (admin-ui/**, AdminAppController.php, built assets) that a patch may not carry cleanly
  git add <specific intended files> && git commit -m "feat: admin SPA scaffold + shell route + auth (0.4)"
  git push -u origin feat/0-4-admin-spa-scaffold && unset GITHUB_TOKEN && gh pr create --fill
  ```
  NOTE: for a brand-new directory tree like `admin-ui/**`, a patch may be awkward — it is OFTEN CLEANER to `cp -r` the new files into the fresh clone and `git add` them there, then re-apply edits to the 2 modified entry-point files via `git apply` or manual edit. Use your judgment; verify `git status` in the clone shows ONLY the intended files before committing.
- Poll `unset GITHUB_TOKEN && gh pr checks <n>` until the FUNCTIONAL checks (PHPUnit, PHPStan, Psalm, PHPCS, Security Audit, Component Tests, and any admin-ui/Vitest job) are GREEN. **NON-blocking CI noise to IGNORE** (do not let it block; PR will read MERGEABLE/UNSTABLE): "Build and Push" Docker jobs (fail on same-repo PRs, no ghcr token), Codacy (ACTION_REQUIRED), codecov/project. Do NOT use `--admin` to override a genuinely BLOCKED required check — if a required check is red, fix it.
- `unset GITHUB_TOKEN && gh pr merge <n> --squash --delete-branch`, then in the LIVE repo reconcile: `git checkout -- <merged files>` (drop now-upstream duplicate edits), `git checkout master && git pull`. Remove the /tmp clone + patches. Repeat for the phlix-docs PR if any.
- Then set the STATUS row 0.4 to `done` with the PR URL(s) + coverage% (edit `/home/sites/phlix/PHLIX_UI_STATUS.md`, the `| 0.4 | ... | todo |` row; update intermediate states as you go: implementing→review→fixing→testing→documenting→merging→done).

## CUMULATIVE pass (after 0.4 is merged):
Spawn **Reviewer (cumulative)** = `code-reviewer` with cross-step scope: review 0.4 together with the merged 0.1/0.2/0.3 foundation — focus on integration + regressions (does the SPA shell route coexist with the coroutine runtime from 0.2 and the existing SSR admin pages? any broken dispatch, drifted docs, coverage gaps at the seams?). Read the completed worklogs + merged diffs. If it finds issues, spawn **Fixer (cumulative)** = `php-implementer`, ⟲ loop until clean; ship fixes via their OWN git cycle and re-run `test-engineer` if code changed. (Note: the 0.3 worklog records the 0.1+0.2+0.3 cumulative was intentionally skipped as 0.3 was isolated infra — so your cumulative scope is effectively "does 0.4 integrate cleanly with the runtime + existing admin"; keep it focused, don't re-litigate 0.3.)

## CARDINAL RULES (non-negotiable — embed this VERBATIM in every coding/git-touching subagent prompt you spawn, and tell each agent to propagate it to anything IT spawns):
```
CARDINAL RULES (non-negotiable):
- Read the step spec (/home/sites/phlix/steps/0.4-admin-spa-scaffold.md) AND the worklog
  (/home/sites/phlix/steps/0.4-admin-spa-scaffold.worklog.md) FIRST; append your results to the
  worklog before finishing. Propagate this entire block to anything you spawn.
- Do not stall for clarification — make the most reasonable choice consistent with this brief, record
  it in the worklog, and report.
- `unset GITHUB_TOKEN` in the SAME command as any `gh` call. `git pull` before any push.
- Work on a `feat/0-4-admin-spa-scaffold` branch; NEVER commit to master directly. Conventional-Commit
  messages + Co-Authored-By trailer (Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>). ONE repo per PR.
- Stage SPECIFIC files (`git add path/to/file`), NEVER `git add -A`/`.`. NEVER `--amend`, force-push,
  or `--no-verify`.
- NEVER stage or delete `.caliber/`, `.agents/`, `.claude/` (auto-gen tooling state). If the caliber
  pre-commit hook corrupts the index or pollutes the commit, use the throwaway-clone flow — do not
  `--no-verify`. (If the live index is corrupt: `rm .git/index && git read-tree HEAD` to heal — history is intact.)
- Verify before claiming done: run the exact verification commands and paste REAL output, not claims.
```

## HARD RUNTIME RULES (Workerman/Webman = resident memory, NOT PHP-FPM — embed in impl/review/test prompts):
- Never `exit`/`die` in handlers; never blocking `sleep()` (use `Workerman\Timer::sleep`); never store request data in `static`/`global` — use `support\Context` (or the existing `Phlix\Server\Http\RequestContext` wrapper). Unbounded `static` arrays leak memory.
- Everything async: new I/O must be non-blocking (coroutine + `workerman/http-client`, or rely on the Swoole runtime hook). DB via the async client/pool. Long jobs go to a queue/worker process, never inline in an HTTP handler. (0.4 is mostly SPA + a thin shell controller, so this is light — but the AdminAppController must not block.)
- Prefer Webman/Workerman-native mechanisms (routing, middleware). The new admin shell route + dispatch should follow the existing `PluginAdminPageController` precedent and AdminMiddleware gating.
- If a step is ambiguous or bigger than ~1 PR, record the open question in the worklog + STATUS and make the smallest reasonable choice — do NOT guess at large architecture.

## RETURN (your checkpoint report to the Master Coordinator — keep it under ~400 words):
- Final state of step 0.4 (done / blocked + why).
- The phlix-server PR URL (+ phlix-docs PR URL if any), merge status, and coverage% achieved.
- Whether the cumulative pass found + fixed anything.
- Confirmation that phlix-server (and phlix-docs if touched) are back on clean+green `master`.
- Any decisions you made (e.g. commit-built-assets vs build-in-CI) and any carry-overs/known issues for the next step.
- The next step to run (0.5) and any prerequisites it needs.

Begin now: read the four files, create the worklog, then run the per-step cycle for 0.4.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/PHLIX_UI_PLAN.md","content":"# Phlix — UI Coverage Plan (server + hub)

> **Authoritative plan.** Executed by an agent hierarchy (OpenCode + MiniMax M2.7). Start via
> `coordinator_prompt.md`. Live progress is tracked in `PHLIX_UI_STATUS.md`. Per-step specs live in
> `steps/<step-id>.md`.

## Context

The Phlix backend is feature-rich and recently driven to "fully working" (see `missing.md` /
`phlix_update.md`): ~132 REST routes across ~30 subsystems. But the **web UI is a thin slice**
of that surface. The server portal renders only browse/playback pages (home, library browse,
music/books/audiobooks/photo, search, player), a **read-only** admin dashboard, and a single
`/admin/plugins` page. Nearly every operator/admin/config capability is **API-only** or
**env/file-only with no UI at all**. The hub UI is similarly sparse (auth, my-servers,
claim-server, requests, shares, invite-accept) and is missing management for the very things it
stores. The `admin-nav.tpl` template even carries a TODO: *"subsequent admin pages (users,
libraries, settings) plug in here."*

Goal: ensure **every supported feature has a UI element to control/view it**. This plan maps the
full backend feature set to its UI status and lays out the work to close the gaps.

**Decisions already captured (from clarifying questions + follow-up messages):**
- **Scope:** both server and hub, **server-first**.
- **Approach:** build a **new client-side admin app** (SPA), not the existing SSR-only pattern.
- **Priority:** **operator control-plane first** (libraries, users, settings, integrations), then
  devices/casting, then viewer-facing polish.
- **Also required:** update `phlix-docs` + each repo's `README.md`; write tests for everything,
  targeting **as-high-as-possible (near-100%) coverage**; **evaluate what belongs in `phlix-shared`**.
- **Runtime:** upgrade to **Webman 2.2 / Workerman 5.1**, make **everything async** (

## Objective
Stand up the React+TypeScript+Vite admin SPA, served at `/admin/*` behind admin auth, talking to the
existing JSON API — an empty but working shell with nav, routing, a typed API client, and shared
components. No feature pages yet.

## Repo
`phlix-server`.

- `phlix-server/admin-ui/**` — new Vite+React+TS project (package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig,
  `src/` with App shell, router, nav, a typed API client wrapping `/api/v1/*` that reuses the JWT
  from `public/assets/js/api-client.js` token storage, and shared components: DataTable, Form, Modal,
  Toast). Build output → `phlix-server/public/assets/admin/`.
- `phlix-server/src/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/AdminAppController.php` — serves the SPA HTML shell
  (loads the built bundle) for `/admin` and `/admin/*`; AdminMiddleware-gated; non-admin → redirect
  `/login`.
- `phlix-server/public/index.php` AND `src/Server/Http/HttpHandler.php` — dispatch `/admin` + `/admin/*`
  to `AdminAppController` (keep existing `/admin/plugins` SSR working until folded in later).
- `phlix-server/.gitignore` — ignore `admin-ui/node_modules`, keep built `public/assets/admin/`
  (decide: commit build output OR build in CI — document the choice).

## Out of scope
- No feature pages (libraries/users/settings) — those are Phase 1.
- Do not remove the existing SSR `/admin/plugins` or `/admin/dashboard` yet.

## Depends on
- 0.1 `done`. (0.2/0.3 not strictly required for the SPA but should land first per phase order.)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `cd admin-ui && npm install && npm run build` produces a bundle in `public/assets/admin/`.
- [ ] Visiting `/admin` as an authenticated admin renders the shell (nav + empty dashboard route);
      a non-admin is redirected to `/login`.

 *

 * coroutine-safe replacement for the static/global pattern under the
 * Workerman 5 + Swoole eventLoop runtime introduced in step 0.2; see
 * `phlix-docs/dev/coroutine-runtime.md` for the no-static-state rule.

# Step 0.4 — Admin SPA scaffold + shell route + auth — Worklog

## Phase 0 Coordinator — pre-dispatch prep (2026-05-27)

### Resume point confirmed
- STATUS rows 0.1, 0.2 (+0.2a/b/c), 0.3 = `done`. First non-done Phase-0 row = **0.4** → correct resume point.
- `phlix-server` verified on clean, pulled `master`: working tree clean, HEAD
  `0936dd1 build: bare-metal Swoole + php-uv install, preflight, and CI loading (0.3) (#129)`,
  `git pull --ff-only` = "Already up to date".
- This is a phlix-server-only step. phlix-docs gets a SEPARATE PR (one repo per PR).

### Execution-model resolution (carried over from 0.3 + applied here)
The mandated per-step cycle says to spawn FRESH subagents (php-implementer, code-reviewer,
test-engineer, scribe) synchronously for each role. **Tool availability check in this environment
(re-verified for 0.4):** `ToolSearch select:Agent,TaskCreate,Task` → "No matching deferred tools
found"; broad search returns only `TaskStop`, `Monitor`, `EnterWorktree`, `ExitWorktree` (background
bash + git worktrees). There is NO Agent/Task/subagent-spawning tool available to me as a subagent —
**identical to the blocker the 0.3 coordinator recorded.**

Per the 0.3 worklog "Execution-model resolution" entry, detain resolved this: the driving session
performs the role work directly (only *nested* subagent spawning is unavailable). 0.3 shipped that
way and is `done`+green. Per my Cardinal Rules ("Do not stall for clarification — make the most
reasonable choice consistent with this brief, record it, and report"), I follow that precedent:
I execute each role (Implementer → Reviewer → Fixer ⟲ → TestEngineer ⟲ → Scribe → cumulative
Reviewer/Fixer) inline as distinct, rigorous phases, recording each as its own worklog section, and
running the EXACT verification commands with REAL output before claiming done. The QA cycle's rigor
(separate review pass, fix loop, real test runs, separate docs pass) is preserved; only the
process-isolation of "fresh subagent per role" is unavailable and is substituted by sequential
in-session phases.

### Environment facts gathered (for the Implementer phase)
- **Auth mechanism to REUSE** (`public/assets/js/api-client.js`): `localStorage` keys
  `access_token` / `refresh_token` / `user`; `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`; on 401 it
  POSTs `{refresh_token}` to `/auth/refresh`, stores the new `access_token` (+ `refresh_token` if
  returned), retries once. The SPA's typed API client must mirror THIS contract exactly — same
  localStorage keys, same Bearer header, same refresh endpoint+shape, same single-retry-on-401.
  `/api/v1/auth/me` is the authed endpoint to consume for "show the user".
- **AdminMiddleware** (`src/Server/Http/Middleware/AdminMiddleware.php`) exposes a reusable
  `checkAccess(Request): ?int` → returns `401` (no/invalid user), `403` (not admin, also audit-logs),
  or `null` (allow). The SSR `/admin/plugins` + `/admin/dashboard` branches already use it. The new
  `AdminAppController` MUST reuse this same gate (single source of truth) and — per the spec —
  redirect non-admins to `/login` (302) rather than rendering a 401/403 HTML body, since the spec's
  acceptance criterion is "a non-admin is redirected to `/login`". (Decision: 401 AND 403 both →
  302 redirect to `/login` for the SPA shell route, because the browser shell can't display a JSON
  error and the user needs to authenticate/escalate via the login flow; recorded here.)
- **TWO entry points** dispatch page routes (spec says `public/index.php` + `HttpHandler.php`; the
  ACTUAL second file is `src/Server/Workerman/HttpHandler.php`, NOT `src/Server/Http/HttpHandler.php`
  — the spec text is slightly off; wire the real one):
  - `public/index.php` — `else { ... }` page-route chain (lines ~149-280+): `/` `/login`
    `str_starts_with($path,'/admin/plugins')` `=== '/admin/dashboard'` `/music` `/books` ...
  - `src/Server/Workerman/HttpHandler.php::dispatch()` (lines ~234-281): same chain via
    `if (...) return ...;`, with helpers `dispatchAdminPlugins()` / `dispatchAdminDashboard()`.
  - **CRITICAL ordering:** the new `/admin` + `/admin/*` SPA catch-all MUST be placed AFTER the
    existing `/admin/plugins` and `/admin/dashboard` branches in BOTH files, so those specific SSR
    routes keep winning (spec: "keep existing SSR /admin/plugins + /admin/dashboard working"). A bare
    `str_starts_with($path,'/admin')` placed before them would hijack the SSR routes.
- **SSR precedent** = `PluginAdminPageController` (constructed `new PluginAdminPageController($loader,
  $publicRoot.'/templates')`, returns `(new Response())->html(...)`). The new `AdminAppController`
  should follow the same thin-controller shape: gate via AdminMiddleware::checkAccess, else read the
  built `public/assets/admin/index.html` shell and return it as an HTML Response. Place it in
  `src/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/AdminAppController.php` per the spec/plan.
- **PHP test precedent** for page controllers:
  `tests/Unit/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/PluginAdminPageControllerTest.php` (+ Book/Audiobook/
  Photo/Music siblings). The AdminAppController test follows this pattern: returns the shell HTML
  (200) for an admin; 302 → /login for a non-admin (mock UserRepository::findAdminById → null) and
  for an unauthenticated request (userId null).
- **node/npm availability:** this sandbox blocks any command whose text contains the literal strings
  "node"/"npm" (the Bash tool denied every probe — `node --version`, `command -v node`,
  `ls .../node`, even via variables). So I could NOT pre-verify the toolchain version. It WILL be
  exercised for real in the Implementer/TestEngineer phases via the mandated
  `cd admin-ui && npm install && npm run build && npm run test` (which must run with
  dangerouslyDisableSandbox or via a path that the policy allows) and, decisively, in PR CI.
  If the local toolchain truly can't run, the build-output decision below (commit built assets) means
  the SERVER side is still fully testable (phpunit) and the SPA is verified in CI.

### Build-output decision (commit built assets vs build-in-CI) — DECIDED
**Commit the built bundle** to `public/assets/admin/` and `.gitignore` `admin-ui/node_modules` (and
`admin-ui/dist` if Vite emits an intermediate). Rationale (matches the coordinator's recommendation):
the production server (Workerman, deployed from source under systemd per 0.3) then has NO Node build
dependency at runtime — `AdminAppController` just serves the static shell + the bundle is served by
the existing static-file handler / nginx `try_files`. CI still runs `npm install && npm run build &&
npm run test` to PROVE the committed bundle is reproducible + tests pass, but the committed artifact
is the source of truth the server ships. This is recorded in the README + phlix-docs dev page.

---

DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR) || ! is_file($real) ) { return (new Response()) ->status(503) ->html( '

503 — Admin UI not built

' . '

The admin SPA bundle is missing. ' . 'Run cd admin-ui && npm install && npm run build.

' ); } $html = file_get_contents($real); if ($html === false) { return (new Response()) ->status(503) ->html('

503 — Admin UI could not be read

'); } return (new Response())->html($html); } /** * Map an {@see AdminMiddleware::checkAccess()} result to the SPA's * response. A `null` gate result means "allowed" and the caller should * render {@see self::shell()}; a non-null result (401 or 403) is mapped * to a 302 redirect to `/login` for both entry points. * * Shared so the redirect-on-deny behaviour for the SPA shell route is * defined in exactly one place. * * @param int|null $gateStatus The value returned by * {@see AdminMiddleware::checkAccess()}. * * @return Response|null `null` when the request is allowed (caller * renders the shell); otherwise a 302 redirect * to `/login`. * * @since 0.11.0 (Step 0.4) */ public function gateRedirect(?int $gateStatus): ?Response { if ($gateStatus === null) { return null; } // Both 401 (unauthenticated) and 403 (authenticated non-admin) send // the browser to the SSR login page, where the user authenticates // / escalates. The SPA shell cannot render a JSON error envelope. return (new Response())->redirect('/login', 302); } }

' . '' . '', ); $controller = new AdminAppController($root); $response = $controller->shell($this->makeRequest(), []); $this->assertSame(200, $response->statusCode); $this->assertStringContainsString('
', $response->body); $this->assertStringContainsString('/assets/admin/assets/index-abc.js', $response->body); $this->assertSame('text/html; charset=utf-8', $response->headers['Content-Type']); } public function test_shell_returns_503_when_the_bundle_is_not_built(): void { // A real, empty public root with no assets/admin/index.html. $root = sys_get_temp_dir() . '/phlix_admin_no_bundle_' . uniqid('', true); mkdir($root, 0o775, true); $this->publicRoot = $root; $controller = new AdminAppController($root); $response = $controller->shell($this->makeRequest(), []); $this->assertSame(503, $response->statusCode); $this->assertStringContainsString('Admin UI not built', $response->body); $this->assertStringContainsString('npm run build', $response->body); } public function test_gateRedirect_returns_null_when_allowed(): void { $controller = new AdminAppController(sys_get_temp_dir()); $this->assertNull($controller->gateRedirect(null)); } public function test_gateRedirect_redirects_unauthenticated_to_login(): void { $controller = new AdminAppController(sys_get_temp_dir()); $response = $controller->gateRedirect(401); $this->assertNotNull($response); $this->assertSame(302, $response->statusCode); $this->assertSame('/login', $response->headers['Location']); } public function test_gateRedirect_redirects_non_admin_to_login(): void { $controller = new AdminAppController(sys_get_temp_dir()); $response = $controller->gateRedirect(403); $this->assertNotNull($response); $this->assertSame(302, $response->statusCode); $this->assertSame('/login', $response->headers['Location']); } /** * Build a temp public root containing assets/admin/index.html with the * given HTML. Records the root for teardown. */ private function makePublicRootWithShell(string $html): string { $root = sys_get_temp_dir() . '/phlix_admin_root_' . uniqid('', true); $adminDir = $root . '/assets/admin'; mkdir($adminDir, 0o775, true); file_put_contents($adminDir . '/index.html', $html); $this->publicRoot = $root; return $root; } private function makeRequest(): Request { $request = new Request(); $request->method = 'GET'; $request->path = '/admin'; $request->headers = []; $request->query = []; $request->body = []; $request->files = []; $request->remoteIp = '127.0.0.1'; $request->remotePort = 0; $request->protocol = 'HTTP/1.1'; $request->queryString = ''; $request->userId = 'admin-1'; return $request; } /** Recursively remove a directory tree created by the test. */ private function rrmdir(string $dir): void { $items = scandir($dir); if ($items === false) { return; } foreach ($items as $item) { if ($item === '.' || $item === '..') { continue; } $path = $dir . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR . $item; if (is_dir($path)) { $this->rrmdir($path); } else { unlink($path); } } rmdir($dir); } }

is the source of truth the server ships. This is recorded in the README + phlix-docs dev page. --- ## Implementer @ 0.4 (2026-05-27) ### Files created (SPA — `admin-ui/`) - `package.json` — React 18 + react-router-dom 6 + TS 5 + Vite 5 + Vitest 2 + @testing-library; scripts: `dev`, `build` (`tsc --noEmit && vite build`), `test` (`vitest run`), `test:coverage`. - `vite.config.ts` — `base: '/assets/admin/'`; `build.outDir` = `../public/assets/admin` (builds straight into the server public dir, the committed artifact); `emptyOutDir`; sourcemaps; Vitest jsdom config + v8 coverage (excludes `main.tsx`/test scaffolding). - `tsconfig.json` + `tsconfig.node.json` — strict, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, react-jsx. - `index.html` — Vite entry; `
` + module script; `noindex` robots. - `src/api/tokenStore.ts` — `TokenStore` interface + `LocalStorageTokenStore` using the EXACT legacy keys `access_token`/`refresh_token`/`user` (cross-compatible with the SSR login). Constants exported. - `src/api/client.ts` — `ApiClient` (typed) mirroring the legacy `api-client.js` contract: Bearer header, single-retry-on-401 via `POST /auth/refresh {refresh_token}`, `ApiError` with status, error-message extraction (`error` → `message` → `Request failed`). Injectable baseUrl/tokenStore/ fetch (testable). `getCurrentUser()` → `GET /api/v1/auth/me`; `isLoggedIn()`; `logout()`. - `src/components/{Toast,Modal,DataTable,Form}.tsx` — the four shared components. All render untrusted values as text/React children (no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`) — XSS-safe by construction. - `src/auth/useAdminGuard.ts` — client-side guard (complements server AdminMiddleware): probes `auth/me`, authorizes `is_admin===true`, else redirects to `/login`; injectable redirect (testable). - `src/nav/{navItems.ts,AdminNav.tsx}` — nav source-of-truth (Dashboard only for 0.4) + sidebar. - `src/pages/{DashboardPage,NotFoundPage}.tsx` — empty dashboard route + client 404. - `src/App.tsx` — shell: guard → loading/redirect/authorized; ToastProvider + nav + routed content. - `src/main.tsx` — bootstrap under `` (bundle mounts at `/admin/*`, assets at `/assets/admin/`). Excluded from coverage (entry only). - `src/styles.css` — minimal scaffold styles. - Tests (Vitest): `api/client.test.ts` (auth header present/absent, body serialization per verb, refresh+retry happy path, no-refresh-token, failed-refresh, refresh keeps old refresh token, network-error refresh=false, error handling incl. text body, helpers), `api/tokenStore.test.ts`, `auth/useAdminGuard.test.tsx` (authorized / no-token / non-admin / 401-403 / loading), `App.test.tsx` (loading, nav+dashboard for admin, renders-nothing+redirect for non-admin, client 404), `components/{Toast,Modal,DataTable,Form}.test.tsx`, `pages/pages.test.tsx`. Test harness: `test/setup.ts` (jest-dom + localStorage clear), `test/memoryTokenStore.ts` (in-memory store + ordered-response fetch mock). ### Files created (PHP) - `src/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/AdminAppController.php` — thin controller (mirrors `PluginAdminPageController` precedent). `shell()` reads the committed `public/assets/admin/index.html` and returns it as HTML (200); 503 with an actionable "run npm run build" message when the bundle is absent (path-traversal-guarded via realpath + str_starts_with under publicRoot). `gateRedirect(?int)` centralises the gate→response mapping: `null`→null (allow), 401/403→302 redirect to `/login`. No DB, no blocking I/O (resident-memory safe). - `tests/Unit/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/AdminAppControllerTest.php` — shell-present (200 + bundle HTML + content-type), shell-missing (503), gateRedirect null/401/403. Uses temp public roots (created+torn down), matches the sibling test conventions (`Response::$statusCode`/`$body`/ `$headers['Location']`). ### Files modified (PHP — both entry points) - `public/index.php` — added `use AdminAppController;` + an `elseif ($path === '/admin' || str_starts_with($path,'/admin/'))` branch placed AFTER `/admin/plugins` and `/admin/dashboard` (so those SSR routes keep winning), gating via `AdminMiddleware::checkAccess` → `gateRedirect` → `shell`. `new AdminAppController(__DIR__)` (here `__DIR__` = public root, consistent with the sibling `PluginAdminPageController(__DIR__.'/templates')`). - `src/Server/Workerman/HttpHandler.php` (NOTE: the spec said `src/Server/Http/HttpHandler.php` — that file does NOT exist; the real Workerman dispatcher is `src/Server/Workerman/HttpHandler.php`). Added `use AdminAppController;`, a `dispatch()` branch (after `/admin/dashboard`, before `/music`), and a private `dispatchAdminApp()` helper using `$this->publicRoot`. Static-file `serveStatic()` already serves `public/assets/admin/*` with correct MIME types, so the JS/CSS bundle needs no extra wiring. - `.gitignore` — ignore `admin-ui/node_modules/`, `admin-ui/coverage/`, `admin-ui/.vite/`, `admin-ui/*.tsbuildinfo`; explicit comment that the built `public/assets/admin/` IS committed. ### Local verification ATTEMPTED — `php -l` PASS; test/build/PR toolchain BLOCKED by environment - `php -l` (PASS, real output): - `No syntax errors detected in .../AdminAppController.php` - `No syntax errors detected in .../Workerman/HttpHandler.php` - `No syntax errors detected in .../public/index.php` - `No syntax errors detected in .../AdminAppControllerTest.php` - **BLOCKED (the Bash tool denies these commands in this environment — permission, not a flake):** every invocation of `node`/`npm` (so `npm install`/`npm run build`/`npm run test` cannot run → the SPA bundle cannot be built locally and Vitest cannot run locally), every invocation of `vendor/bin/phpunit` / `vendor/bin/phpstan` / `vendor/bin/phpcs` (even `phpunit --version`), and `gh` (so a PR cannot be opened from here). Read-only `git` works; `php -l` works. This is the SAME class of environment limitation the 0.3 coordinator hit with subagent-spawning, but broader: here the **mandated verification commands themselves and the PR-delivery command are unavailable to me.** The git working tree shows EXACTLY the intended delta (`git status --short`): `M .gitignore`, `M public/index.php`, `M src/Server/Workerman/HttpHandler.php`, `?? admin-ui/`, `?? src/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/AdminAppController.php`, `?? tests/Unit/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/AdminAppControllerTest.php` — no stray files. --- ## Reviewer @ 0.4 (static self-review — no runtime; tool-blocked) (2026-05-27) Since the test/static-analysis runners are blocked, I performed a careful READ-only review of the full diff against the Acceptance criteria + security + async/resident-memory rules + Webman/repo conventions. Findings: 1. **Acceptance — build output (criterion 1):** `vite.config.ts build.outDir` resolves to `../public/assets/admin` so `npm run build` will emit the bundle into `public/assets/admin/` as required. CANNOT be runtime-confirmed locally (npm blocked) → must be confirmed by CI / a human build. NOT a code defect. 2. **Acceptance — shell + non-admin redirect (criterion 2):** both entry points gate via `AdminMiddleware::checkAccess` and map 401/403→302 `/login` via `gateRedirect`; admin path renders the shell. Matches spec ("non-admin redirected to /login"). Covered by AdminAppControllerTest + App.test.tsx. 3. **Acceptance — typed client + auth/me (criterion 3):** `ApiClient.getCurrentUser()` → `GET /api/v1/auth/me`, Bearer header, refresh+retry. Mirrors legacy `api-client.js` exactly (same keys/endpoint/shape) — no new auth invented. Covered by client.test.ts. 4. **Acceptance — Vitest (criterion 4):** API-client unit tests + shell render test + guard tests present (exceeds the "at least API-client + shell render" floor). CANNOT run locally (npm blocked). 5. **Acceptance — entry-point dispatch + SSR preserved (criterion 5):** SPA branch placed AFTER `/admin/plugins` + `/admin/dashboard` in BOTH files → existing SSR routes keep winning. Verified by re-reading both files. 6. **Security:** (a) shell `realpath` + `str_starts_with($real, publicRoot.SEP)` guards path traversal (the shell path is a fixed constant, so this is defence-in-depth). (b) All React render paths use text/children, never `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` → XSS-safe; a dedicated Toast test asserts an `` payload is rendered as literal text. (c) Auth uses the existing JWT Bearer scheme; no token in URL; AdminMiddleware (server) remains the authoritative gate, the SPA guard is defensive-only. (d) No SQL in this step. 7. **Async / resident-memory:** AdminAppController does only `realpath`/`is_file`/`file_get_contents` on a local committed file — no DB, no network, no blocking remote I/O, no `sleep`, no `exit`/`die`, no static/global request state. Resident-mem safe. (Possible micro-optimisation for a later phase: cache the shell HTML in memory; NOT needed for the scaffold — left out deliberately.) 8. **Conventions:** PSR-12 style + `declare(strict_types=1)` + full docblocks (`@since 0.11.0 (Step 0.4)`), namespace mirrors dir, `final class`, readonly ctor promotion — matches the sibling controllers. CANNOT run phpcs/phpstan locally to confirm L9/PSR-12 → CI must confirm. **NO blocking code findings.** The only open items are the THREE verifications that require the blocked toolchain (build emits bundle; Vitest green; phpunit/phpstan-L9/phpcs green) — these are exactly what CI runs, so they will be confirmed when the PR's CI executes. No Fixer pass needed on code grounds. --- ## BLOCKER (Phase 0 Coordinator) — 2026-05-27 **Cannot drive step 0.4 to `done` in this environment: the mandated verification commands AND the PR-delivery command are blocked.** The code is COMPLETE and statically clean (`php -l` passes on all PHP; the SPA + tests are written to spec and self-reviewed with no blocking findings). But step 0.4 contains real PHP + a real SPA + Vitest tests, and the step is explicitly "not done until [the verification commands] are GREEN — paste REAL output". In THIS environment the Bash tool denies: - `npm install` / `npm run build` / `npm run test` (and any `node`/`npm`/`nvm` command) → the SPA bundle cannot be built locally (so the committed `public/assets/admin/` artifact, per the build-output decision, cannot be produced here) and Vitest cannot run. - `vendor/bin/phpunit` / `phpstan` / `phpcs` (even `--version`) → PHP suite + L9 + PSR-12 cannot run. - `gh` → the PR cannot be created/merged from here. Read-only `git` and `php -l` DO work. I am NOT working around these denials (e.g. obfuscating `node`) — they are deliberate guardrails. Per the per-step QA cycle ("the step is not done until these are GREEN") and the Cardinal Rule ("Verify before claiming done: run the exact verification commands and paste REAL output, not claims"), I cannot truthfully mark 0.4 `done` without that real green output, and I cannot open the PR that would let CI produce it. **State:** all 0.4 code written + statically clean + self-reviewed (no blocking findings); working tree shows only the intended delta; nothing committed; phlix-server remains on clean, pulled `master`. STATUS row 0.4 set to `blocked` with this reason. **What the Master Coordinator (or detain) needs to do to unblock — ONE of:** 1. Enable `npm`/`node`, `vendor/bin/{phpunit,phpstan,phpcs}`, and `gh` for this agent (lift the Bash content-block on those commands) so I can run the verification commands, build+commit the bundle, and open/merge the PR per the git cycle; OR 2. Have the top-level session / a human run the three verification command blocks + the build, then hand me the real output (and either commit the built bundle or approve "build-in-CI" so the bundle is produced by the CI workflow instead of committed — see decision contingency below); OR 3. Re-assign 0.4's verify+git-cycle to a harness/session that has those commands (the code in the working tree is ready to verify + ship as-is). **Decision contingency (build-output):** the worklog records "commit the built bundle". That requires a successful `npm run build`. If the bundle cannot be built+committed by whoever unblocks this, the clean fallback is **build-in-CI**: add an `admin-ui` build+test job to `.github/workflows/` that runs `npm ci && npm run build && npm run test`, and EITHER commit the produced bundle from CI or have the deploy build it. Either way `AdminAppController::shell()` already returns an actionable 503 if the bundle is missing at runtime, so a server without the bundle fails loud, not silent. ---

## Objective
Stand up the React+TypeScript+Vite admin SPA, served at `/admin/*` behind admin auth, talking to the
existing JSON API — an empty but working shell with nav, routing, a typed API client, and shared
components. No feature pages yet.

## Repo
`phlix-server`.

## In scope (files)
- `phlix-server/admin-ui/**` — new Vite+React+TS project (package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig,
  `src/` with App shell, router, nav, a typed API client wrapping `/api/v1/*` that reuses the JWT
  from `public/assets/js/api-client.js` token storage, and shared components: DataTable, Form, Modal,
  Toast). Build output → `phlix-server/public/assets/admin/`.
- `phlix-server/src/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/AdminAppController.php` — serves the SPA HTML shell
  (loads the built bundle) for `/admin` and `/admin/*`; AdminMiddleware-gated; non-admin → redirect
  `/login`.
- `phlix-server/public/index.php` AND `src/Server/Http/HttpHandler.php` — dispatch `/admin` + `/admin/*`
  to `AdminAppController` (keep existing `/admin/plugins` SSR working until folded in later).
- `phlix-server/.gitignore` — ignore `admin-ui/node_modules`, keep built `public/assets/admin/`
  (decide: commit build output OR build in CI — document the choice).

## Out of scope
- No feature pages (libraries/users/settings) — those are Phase 1.
- Do not remove the existing SSR `/admin/plugins` or `/admin/dashboard` yet.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-server/README.md","content":"# Phlix Media Server

[![PHPUnit](https://github.com/detain/phlix-server/actions/workflows/phpunit.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/detain/phlix-server/actions/workflows/phpunit.yml)
[![Coding Standards](https://github.com/detain/phlix-server/actions/workflows/coding-standards.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/detain/phlix-server/actions/workflows/coding-standards.yml)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/detain/phlix-server/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/detain/phlix-server)
[![PHP](https://img.shields.io/badge/PHP-8.3%2B-777bb4?logo=php&logoColor=white)](https://www.php.net/)
[![PHPStan](https://img.shields.io/badge/PHPStan-level%209-brightgreen)](https://phpstan.org/)
[![Code style](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-PSR--12-blueviolet)](https://www.php-fig.org/psr/psr-12/)

A comprehensive media server platform built with PHP 8.3+, featuring real-time WebSocket communication, HTTP REST APIs, and support for multiple client platforms including Roku, Samsung Tizen, and Windows.

> **Repository moved 2026-05-17:** this codebase migrated from `github.com/detain/phlix` to [`github.com/detain/phlix-server`](https://github.com/detain/phlix-server) as part of the Phase B repo split (see `PHLIX_EXPANSION_PLAN.md`). Update existing local clones with `git remote set-url origin git@github.com:detain/phlix-server.git`. The old repo is being archived in step B.4b.

## Overview

Phlix Media Server provides a complete media management and streaming solution:

- **Media Library Management**: Organize and browse media collections with automatic scanning
- **User Authentication**: JWT-based auth with refresh tokens
- **Real-time SyncPlay**: Watch content together with friends
- **Live TV Support**: DVR and guide integration
- **DLNA Streaming**: Standard protocol support for compatible devices
- **Transcoding**: On-the-fly media conversion via FFmpeg with a

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-server/CHANGELOG.md","content":"# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and the project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [Unreleased]

### Added

- **Bare-metal Swoole + php-uv build (step 0.3).** `scripts/install.sh` and `install/systemd.sh` now compile the Swoole and php-uv extensions from source as part of a fresh install (and on the `scripts/install.sh --update` repair path), giving the step 0.2 coroutine runtime real extensions on Debian/Ubuntu hosts — not just in Docker. The Swoole `./configure` flag set is copied **verbatim** from `docker/Dockerfile.base` (see `docker/README.md` "Swoole build flags" for the per-flag rationale); php-uv is built with `--with-uv`. The apt `-dev` build dependencies (`build-essential autoconf pkg-config git libssl-dev libuv1-dev libbrotli-dev libzstd-dev libnghttp2-dev libpq-dev libsqlite3-dev libc-ares-dev liburing-dev libssh2-1-dev`, plus the version-matched `phpX.Y-dev` for `phpize`) are the Debian translation of the Alpine set. The build is **idempotent**: each step short-circuits via `php -m` when the extension already loads, so re-running the installer never triggers the slow recompile. `--enable-iouring` / `--enable-uring-socket` build on any kernel but only activate at runtime on Linux kernel ≥ 5.6 (older kernels fall back to epoll automatically).
- **Workerman disable-function preflight (step 0.3).** A new preflight in both installers fails loudly and early if `disable_functions` blocks any process-control / posix / socket primitive Workerman needs to fork workers and manage sockets (`pcntl_*`, `posix_*`, `proc_*`, `exec`/`shell_exec`, `stream_socket_*`), with an actionable message pointing the operator at their `php.ini` (and php-fpm pool config) — instead of a cryptic runtime crash aft

# Phlix Documentation

[![Deploy Docs](https://github.com/detain/phlix-docs/actions/workflows/docs.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/detain/phlix-docs/actions/workflows/docs.yml)

Phlix Media Server documentation — end-user guides, developer docs, and hub-admin references.

## Live Site

Visit the published docs at: **https://detain.github.io/phlix-docs** (GitHub Pages)

## Local Development

```bash
npm install
npm run docs:dev
```

Then open http://localhost:5173

## Building

```bash
npm run docs:build   # builds to docs/.vitepress/dist
npm run docs:preview # preview the built site
```

## Contributing

Doc PRs are welcome! Please keep changes focused on the markdown content in `docs/`.

For developer docs, see the [Developer Documentation](https://detain.github.io/phlix-docs/dev/architecture-server).
For hub admin guides, see the [Hub Admin Documentation](https://detain.github.io/phlix-docs/hub-admin/install).

# Planned / unwritten pages

This file is a backlog, **not a published doc** (it lives at the repo root, outside `docs/`, so
VitePress does not build it).

The following sidebar/nav entries used to point at pages that were never written, so they 404'd
when clicked. They were removed from `docs/.vitepress/config.ts` to keep the navigation clean.
Re-add the sidebar entry (and the homepage card / cross-links if relevant) once the page exists.

| Suggested route | Title | Section (sidebar key) | Intended content |
|-----------------|-------|------------------------|------------------|
| `docs/faq.md` | FAQ | `/faq` (standalone) | Frequently asked questions for end users |
| `docs/reference/config-files.md` | Config Files | `/reference/` | Reference for the server/hub `config/*.php` files and their keys |
| `docs/advanced/arr-integration.md` | ARR Integration | `/advanced/` | User-facing guide to Sonarr/Radarr/etc. integration (dev detail lives in `dev/arr-clients.md`) |
| `docs/dev/test-harness.md` | Test Harness | `/dev/` → Developer Reference | How to run and extend the test suite / harness |
| `docs/dev/debug-recipes.md` | Debug Recipes | `/dev/` → Developer Reference | Common debugging recipes and tooling |
| `docs/dev/release-process.md` | Release Process | `/dev/` → Developer Reference | How releases are cut, versioned, and published |

## How to restore one

1. Create the markdown file at the suggested route.
2. Add its entry back to the matching sidebar group in `docs/.vitepress/config.ts`.
3. Run `npm run docs:build` to confirm it resolves and appears in the sidebar.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-server/src/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/AdminAppController.php","content":"<?php

declare(strict_types=1);

namespace Phlix\Server\WebPortal\Controllers;

use Phlix\Server\Http\Middleware\AdminMiddleware;
use Phlix\Server\Http\Request;
use Phlix\Server\Http\Response;

/**
 * Serves the HTML shell for the client-side admin SPA (step 0.4).
 *
 * The admin console is a React + TypeScript + Vite single-page app whose
 * source lives in `phlix-server/admin-ui/` and whose built bundle is
 * committed to `public/assets/admin/` (see the step 0.4 worklog for the
 * "commit built assets" decision — the production Workerman server then
 * has no Node build dependency at runtime). This controller does one
 * thing: return that built `index.html` shell for `/admin` and any
 * `/admin/*` deep link so client-side routing works on reload. All data
 * is fetched by the SPA from the existing JWT-authed JSON API
 * (`/api/v1/*`); this controller renders no data itself.
 *
 * AuthN/AuthZ mirrors the {@see PluginAdminPageController} precedent: the
 * controller does NOT re-validate the admin role — the caller gates the
 * route with {@see AdminMiddleware::checkAccess()} (single source of truth
 * for the admin gate + audit logging). For the SPA shell, a failed gate
 * (401 unauthenticated OR 403 non-admin) maps to a 302 redirect to
 * `/login` rather than a JSON/HTML error body, because the shell route is
 * a browser navigation and the user must (re-)authenticate via the SSR
 * login flow. {@see self::gateRedirect()} centralises that mapping so both
 * entry points (`public/index.php` and
 * `src/Server/Workerman/HttpHandler.php`) behave identically.
 *
 * The existing SSR `/admin/plugins` and `/admin/dashboard` routes are NOT
 * affected: callers MUST dispatch those specific prefixes BEFORE the
 * generic `/admin` + `/admin/*` SPA catch-all, so they keep winning until
 * they

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-docs/docs/dev/coroutine-runtime.md","content":"# Coroutine runtime (Workerman 5 + Swoole)

> **Audience:** new contributors writing handlers, middleware, controllers,
> or background workers in `phlix-server` and `phlix-hub`.
>
> **Tl;dr.** Both daemons run on Workerman 5 with the Swoole eventLoop
> driver and `Swoole\Runtime::enableCoroutine(SWOOLE_HOOK_ALL)`. **Never
> use `exit`/`die`, never `sleep()`, and never store per-request data in
> `static` properties / `global` / `$GLOBALS`.** Use
> `Phlix\Server\Http\RequestContext` (server) or
> `Phlix\Hub\Http\RequestContext` (hub) for per-request state. Both wrap
> `support\Context`, which the eventLoop isolates per coroutine.

This page covers the runtime model introduced in step 0.2 of the UI
coverage plan. The entry-point Swoole hook landed in step **0.2a**
([`phlix-server` PR #126](https://github.com/detain/phlix-server/pull/126));
the `support\Context` migration + tests + bench + this doc landed in step
**0.2b** (server) and step **0.2c** (hub).

---

## 1. What the runtime does

`phlix-server/start.php` and `phlix-hub/start.php` perform this dance
**before any `Worker` is instantiated**:

```php
if (extension_loaded('swoole')) {
    Worker::$eventLoop = \Workerman\Events\Swoole::class;
    \Swoole\Runtime::enableCoroutine(SWOOLE_HOOK_ALL);
} else {
    trigger_error(
        'Swoole extension not detected — coroutine runtime will not be active. Install ext-swoole to enable.',
        E_USER_WARNING
    );
}
```

What that buys us:

| Setting | Effect |
|---|---|
| `Worker::$eventLoop = \Workerman\Events\Swoole::class` | Workerman uses Swoole's reactor instead of its own select-based loop. Required for coroutines. |
| `Swoole\Runtime::enableCoroutine(SWOOLE_HOOK_ALL)` | PHP's blocking I/O (`file_get_contents`, `curl_exec`, `PDO`, `sleep`/`usleep`/`time_nanosleep`, DNS, sockets, streams, …) is t

### Git Workflow

1. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feature/my-feature`
tool_input.new_string:
### Admin SPA (admin-ui)

The admin console is a **React + TypeScript + Vite** single-page app. Its source lives in
`admin-ui/`; the production build is emitted into `public/assets/admin/` and **committed to the
repo**, so the running Workerman server has **no Node build dependency at runtime** (it just serves
the static shell + bundle). `admin-ui/node_modules/` is gitignored.

The SPA mounts at `/admin` and `/admin/*`, served by `AdminAppController` (returns the built
`index.html` shell; 503 if the bundle is missing) and gated by the existing `AdminMiddleware` — a
non-admin (401/403) is redirected (302) to `/login`. Its typed `ApiClient` reuses the same JWT
mechanism as `public/assets/js/api-client.js` (`access_token`/`refresh_token` in `localStorage`,
Bearer header, single retry on 401 via `POST /auth/refresh`).

```bash
cd admin-ui
npm install          # one-time / on dependency changes
npm run build        # tsc + vite build → ../public/assets/admin/ (commit the result)
npm run test         # Vitest unit/component tests
npm run dev          # Vite dev server (HMR) for local development
```

When you change anything under `admin-ui/src/`, re-run `npm run build` and commit the refreshed
`public/assets/admin/` bundle along with your source changes.

### Git Workflow

1. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feature/my-feature`

- **Bare-metal Swoole + php-uv build (step 0.3).** `scripts/install.sh` and `install/systemd.sh` now compile the Swoole and php-uv extensions from source as part of a fresh install (and on the `scripts/install.sh --update` repair path), giving the step 0.2 coroutine runtime real extensions on Debian/Ubuntu hosts — not just in Docker. The Swoole `./configure` flag set is copied **verbatim** from `docker/Dockerfile.base` (see `docker/README.md` "Swoole build flags" for the per-flag rationale); php-uv is built with `--with-uv`. The apt `-dev` build dependencies (`build-essential autoconf pkg-config git libssl-dev libuv1-dev libbrotli-dev libzstd-dev libnghttp2-dev libpq-dev libsqlite3-dev libc-ares-dev liburing-dev libssh2-1-dev`, plus the version-matched `phpX.Y-dev` for `phpize`) are the Debian translation of the Alpine set. The build is **idempotent**: each step short-circuits via `php -m` when the extension already loads, so re-running the installer never triggers the slow recompile. `--enable-iouring` / `--enable-uring-socket` build on any kernel but only activate at runtime on Linux kernel ≥ 5.6 (older kernels fall back to epoll automatically).
- **Workerman disable-function preflight (step 0.3).** A new preflight in both installers fails loudly and early if `disable_functions` blocks any process-control / posix / socket primitive Workerman needs to fork workers and manage sockets (`pcntl_*`, `posix_*`, `proc_*`, `exec`/`shell_exec`, `stream_socket_*`), with an actionable message pointing the operator at their `php.ini` (and php-fpm pool config) — instead of a cryptic runtime crash aft

tool_input.content:
# Admin SPA (admin-ui)

> **Audience:** contributors working on the `phlix-server` admin console.
>
> **Tl;dr.** The admin console is a **React + TypeScript + Vite** single-page
> app in `phlix-server/admin-ui/`. Its production bundle is built into
> `public/assets/admin/` and **committed to the repo**, so the running
> Workerman server has **no Node build dependency at runtime**. It mounts at
> `/admin` + `/admin/*` behind the existing `AdminMiddleware` (non-admin →
> 302 `/login`) and talks to the existing JWT-authed JSON API. After editing
> `admin-ui/src/`, re-run `npm run build` and commit the refreshed bundle.

This page covers the admin SPA scaffold introduced in step **0.4** of the UI
coverage plan. Step 0.4 ships a working *shell* — nav, router, a typed API
client, and shared components — with **no feature pages yet** (those land in
Phase 1). The viewer-facing portal stays server-side-rendered (Smarty); only
the admin console is a client-side SPA.

---

## 1. Why a client-side SPA for the admin console

The viewer portal (`/`, `/music`, `/books`, …) is server-side-rendered with
Smarty and stays that way: it is largely read-oriented, benefits from fast
first paint and shareable URLs, and is already built. The **admin console** is
the opposite shape — dense, stateful, form-heavy CRUD over libraries, users,
and settings, with tables, modals, and optimistic updates. A client-side SPA
backed by the existing JSON API keeps that interaction local to the browser,
avoids a full-page round-trip per action, and lets the admin surface evolve
without touching server-render code. The two coexist: the SSR routes
(`/admin/plugins`, `/admin/dashboard`) keep working until they are folded into
the SPA in a later phase.

---

## 2. Source layout

The SPA source lives under `phlix-server/admin-ui/`:

| Path | Contents |
|---|---|
| `admin-ui/src/api/` | `client.ts` (the typed `ApiClient`) + `tokenStore.ts` (JWT token storage). |
| `admin-ui/src/auth/` | `useAdminGuard.ts` — the client-side admin guard (defence in depth over the server gate). |
| `admin-ui/src/components/` | Shared components: `DataTable`, `Form`, `Modal`, `Toast`. All render untrusted values as text/children (no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`) — XSS-safe by construction. |
| `admin-ui/src/nav/` | `navItems.ts` (nav source-of-truth) + `AdminNav.tsx` (sidebar). |
| `admin-ui/src/pages/` | `DashboardPage` (empty for 0.4) + `NotFoundPage`. |
| `admin-ui/src/App.tsx` | The shell: guard → loading / redirect / authorized; toast provider + nav + routed content. |
| `admin-ui/src/main.tsx` | Bootstrap under `<BrowserRouter basename="/admin">`. |

Build configuration: `vite.config.ts` sets `base: '/assets/admin/'` and
`build.outDir` to `../public/assets/admin` (Vite builds straight into the
server's public directory). `tsconfig.json` is strict
(`noUncheckedIndexedAccess`).

---

## 3. Dev server vs. production build

```bash
cd admin-ui
npm install          # one-time / on dependency changes
npm run dev          # Vite dev server with HMR for local development
npm run build        # tsc --noEmit + vite build → ../public/assets/admin/
npm run test         # Vitest unit/component tests
```

`npm run dev` gives a hot-reloading dev server. **`npm run build` is what
ships:** it emits the production bundle into `public/assets/admin/`, which is
committed. When you change anything under `admin-ui/src/`, re-run
`npm run build` and commit the refreshed `public/assets/admin/` bundle along
with your source changes — CI runs `npm install && npm run build && npm run
test` to prove the committed bundle is reproducible and the tests pass.

`admin-ui/node_modules/` (and `admin-ui/coverage/`, `admin-ui/.vite/`,
`admin-ui/*.tsbuildinfo`) are gitignored; the built `public/assets/admin/`
directory is **not** ignored — it is the committed artifact the server ships.

---

## 4. The build-output decision (commit the bundle)

The built bundle is **committed** to `public/assets/admin/` rather than built
in CI/at deploy time. Rationale: the production server (Workerman, deployed
from source under systemd) then has **no Node build dependency at runtime** —
`AdminAppController` just serves the static `index.html` shell, and the
JS/CSS bundle is served by the existing static-file handler. CI still runs the
full `npm install && npm run build && npm run test` to prove the committed
artifact is reproducible, but the committed file is the source of truth the
server ships. If the bundle is ever missing at runtime,
`AdminAppController::shell()` returns a loud, actionable 503 rather than a
silent blank page.

---

## 5. Where and how it mounts

The SPA is served by
`src/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/AdminAppController.php`, a thin controller
that:

- `shell()` reads the committed `public/assets/admin/index.html` and returns
  it (200) for `/admin` and any `/admin/*` deep link, so client-side routing
  survives a page reload. It returns **503** (with a "run `npm run build`"
  message) when the bundle is absent, and path-traversal-guards the read
  (`realpath` + `str_starts_with` under the public root) as defence in depth.
- `gateRedirect(?int)` centralises the gate→response mapping: a `null` gate
  result means "allowed" (render the shell); a non-null result (**401**
  unauthenticated or **403** non-admin) maps to a **302 redirect to
  `/login`**, since a browser navigation can't render a JSON error envelope.

The route is wired in **both** entry points — `public/index.php` and
`src/Server/Workerman/HttpHandler.php` — and in both it is placed **AFTER**
the existing `/admin/plugins` and `/admin/dashboard` SSR branches, so those
specific routes keep winning. Each entry point gates the request with the
existing `AdminMiddleware::checkAccess()` (the single source of truth for the
admin gate + audit logging) and maps the result through `gateRedirect()`.

The client-side `useAdminGuard` is **defence in depth only** — the
authoritative gate is the server-side `AdminMiddleware`.

---

## 6. The typed API client and the `auth/me` contract gotcha

`admin-ui/src/api/client.ts` provides a typed `ApiClient` that **reuses the
existing JWT mechanism** from `public/assets/js/api-client.js` — it does not
invent a new auth scheme. Specifically it uses the same `localStorage` keys
(`access_token` / `refresh_token` / `user`), sends `Authorization: Bearer
<access_token>`, and on a 401 does a **single** retry after `POST
/auth/refresh {refresh_token}`.

`getCurrentUser()` consumes `GET /api/v1/auth/me`. **Two contract details bit
the first implementation** (the Vitest mocks initially encoded the wrong
shape, so a green suite still mis-handled the real backend):

1. **The response is wrapped.** `AuthController::me()` returns
   `{ "user": { … } }`, not a flat user object. The client unwraps the
   envelope in `getCurrentUser()` (`const { user } = await
   this.get<{ user: AuthUser }>('/api/v1/auth/me'); return user;`).
2. **`is_admin` is a DB `TINYINT`, not a JSON boolean.** The `users.is_admin`
   column serialises as `1` / `0` (or `"1"` / `"0"`), never JSON `true`. A
   strict `=== true` check fails for a real admin. The client normalises it at
   the API boundary via an exported `normalizeBool()` (treats
   `true` / `1` / `"1"` / `"true"` as true) so the rest of the app sees a real
   boolean.

If you add code that reads `auth/me` (or any endpoint that returns
DB-`TINYINT` flags), mirror this: unwrap envelopes and normalise integer
booleans at the client boundary, and write the Vitest mock to match the
**real** wire shape (`{ user: { …, is_admin: 1 } }`) so the test guards the
true contract.

---

## 7. Tests

Vitest covers the new SPA modules to ~99% statements/lines: the API client
(auth header present/absent, body serialization per verb, refresh-and-retry,
error handling), the token store, `useAdminGuard` (authorized / no-token /
non-admin / `is_admin: 1` envelope / loading / unmount-race), the App shell
(loading, nav + dashboard for an admin, renders-nothing + redirect for a
non-admin, client 404), and the four shared components. The remaining
uncovered lines are real-`window` navigation one-liners
(`window.location.href`) that jsdom cannot exercise.

On the server side, `AdminAppControllerTest` covers the shell (200 + bundle
HTML + content-type), the missing-bundle case (503), and the `gateRedirect`
mapping (null → allow, 401/403 → 302 `/login`).

  commands with the "re-build + commit the bundle after editing src" note.

  unwrap/normalise, and "shell/scaffold only — feature pages are Phase 1" (~99% Vitest coverage +
  AdminAppControllerTest).

**phlix-docs repo (ships in the SEPARATE phlix-docs PR):**

  layout (`admin-ui/src/{api,auth,components,nav,pages}`); dev server vs production build; the
  commit-the-bundle decision (no runtime Node dep); how it mounts (AdminAppController + both entry
  points + AdminMiddleware gate + 302→/login + SSR-routes-win ordering); the `GET /api/v1/auth/me`
  contract gotcha (`{ user: {...} }` envelope + TINYINT `is_admin` `1`/`0` → unwrap + normalizeBool);

  `{ text: 'Admin SPA (admin-ui)', link: '/dev/admin-spa' }` to the `/dev/` "Developer Reference"

**Nav-convention note (discrepancy recorded honestly):** the brief said to mirror the convention
used for `docs/dev/coroutine-runtime.md`. On inspection, `coroutine-runtime.md` exists as a file but
is NOT referenced anywhere in `docs/.vitepress/config.ts` — i.e. the 0.2 Scribe created that page
without ever adding a sidebar entry, so it is reachable only by direct URL. That contradicts the
project's stated convention (`PLANNED_PAGES.md`: real pages SHOULD be listed in the sidebar; only
not-yet-written pages are pulled out to avoid 404s). I therefore made `admin-spa` DISCOVERABLE by
adding it to the `/dev/` sidebar (consistent with the project's intent) rather than replicating the
coroutine-runtime omission. Flagging the coroutine-runtime nav gap as an existing, out-of-scope
oversight that a future docs pass may want to fix.

You are the **Scribe (role E)** for step 0.4 of the Phlix UI Coverage build. Update docs to match EXACTLY what step 0.4 shipped. Make NO behavioral code changes (docblock/comment-only edits in code are allowed, but the 0.4 code already has thorough docblocks — you likely need none). You have Read/Edit/Write/Grep/Glob/Bash; you do NOT need npm/gh.

## Read FIRST
1. `/home/sites/phlix/steps/0.4-admin-spa-scaffold.md` (spec) and `/home/sites/phlix/steps/0.4-admin-spa-scaffold.worklog.md` (the FULL record of what shipped — read it; it has the build-output decision, the file list, and the auth-contract fix).

## What shipped (ground truth — verified GREEN by the coordinator)
A React + TypeScript + Vite admin SPA at `phlix-server/admin-ui/`, built to `phlix-server/public/assets/admin/` (the **built bundle IS committed**; `admin-ui/node_modules` is gitignored — so the production Workerman server has no Node build dependency at runtime). It mounts at `/admin` + `/admin/*`, served by a new `src/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/AdminAppController.php` (returns the built `index.html` shell; 503 if the bundle is missing), gated by the existing `AdminMiddleware::checkAccess()` (401/403 → 302 redirect to `/login`). Dispatch is wired in BOTH entry points: `public/index.php` and `src/Server/Workerman/HttpHandler.php` (placed AFTER the existing `/admin/plugins` + `/admin/dashboard` SSR routes so those keep working). The typed `ApiClient` (`admin-ui/src/api/client.ts`) reuses the EXISTING JWT mechanism from `public/assets/js/api-client.js` (same `localStorage` keys `access_token`/`refresh_token`/`user`, Bearer header, single-retry-on-401 via `POST /auth/refresh {refresh_token}`). `getCurrentUser()` consumes `GET /api/v1/auth/me`, which returns a `{ user: {...} }` envelope with `is_admin` as a DB `TINYINT` (`1`/`0`) — the client unwraps the envelope and normalizes `is_admin` to a real boolean (`normalizeBool`). No feature pages yet (Phase 1). Commands: `cd admin-ui && npm install && npm run build` (build) / `npm run test` (Vitest) / `npm run dev` (Vite dev server). Coverage: 99% on the new SPA modules.

## Deliverables (THREE doc targets)
**A) `phlix-server/README.md`** — add an admin-ui subsection (best fit: under `## Development`, near "### Running Tests"/"### Code Standards", or a new `### Admin SPA (admin-ui)` subsection). Cover: the stack (React+TS+Vite), where the source lives, the build command(s), that the built bundle is committed to `public/assets/admin/` and node_modules is gitignored, where it mounts (`/admin/*`, AdminMiddleware-gated), and how to run the Vitest tests + the Vite dev server. Keep it concise and consistent with the README's existing tone/format.

**B) `phlix-server/CHANGELOG.md`** — add a new bullet under `## [Unreleased]` → `### Added` (follow the exact style of the existing 0.2/0.3 bullets: bold lead-in `**Admin SPA scaffold (step 0.4).**` then a tight prose description). Mention: the React+TS+Vite admin console at `/admin/*` behind AdminMiddleware, the committed-bundle decision, the typed API client reusing the existing JWT mechanism, and that it's a shell/scaffold (no feature pages yet — those are Phase 1).

**C) `phlix-docs/docs/dev/admin-spa.md`** (NEW page — this ships as a SEPARATE phlix-docs PR, NOT in the phlix-server PR) — a developer page: the SPA stack + rationale (why a client-side SPA for the admin console while the viewer portal stays SSR), the source layout (`admin-ui/src/{api,auth,components,nav,pages}`), the dev server (`npm run dev`) vs production build (`npm run build` → committed `public/assets/admin/`), where/how it mounts (AdminAppController + both entry points + AdminMiddleware gate + 302→/login), the build-output decision (commit built bundle; no runtime Node dep), and the `GET /api/v1/auth/me` contract gotcha (responds `{ user: {...} }` with `is_admin` as TINYINT `1`/`0` — the client unwraps + normalizes). Then **add this page to the mkdocs nav**: inspect `phlix-docs/mkdocs.yml` and follow the SAME convention used for the existing `docs/dev/coroutine-runtime.md` entry (find how that page is listed and mirror it). If nav is auto-generated (no manual list), no nav edit is needed — say so in the worklog.

## CARDINAL RULES (non-negotiable):
- Read the step spec + worklog FIRST; append what you documented to the worklog before finishing.
- Do not stall for clarification — make the most reasonable choice consistent with this brief and record it.
- No behavioral code changes. Match docs to what ACTUALLY shipped (per the worklog), do not invent features.

## Objective
Add an admin-only endpoint that lists directories under allowed roots so the "add library" UI can
offer a path picker — safely jailed against traversal.

## Repo
`phlix-server`.

## In scope (files)
- New `phlix-server/src/Server/Http/Controllers/Admin/FsBrowseController.php` —
  `GET /api/v1/admin/fs/browse?path=...` returns the immediate subdirectories of `path`, restricted
  to configured allowed roots.
- Route registration in `src/Server/Core/Application.php` (admin group).
- (Optional) allowed-roots config key in `config/*.php` (e.g. `/home`, `/mnt`, configurable).

## Out of scope
- No UI (Phase 1.1 consumes this).
- Not a general file manager — directories only, no read/write/delete.

## Depends on
- 0.4 (so the picker has a consumer later), but this endpoint stands alone.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Returns subdirectories for a valid path under an allowed root.
- [ ] **Path-traversal safe:** resolve `realpath()` then assert `str_starts_with($real, $allowedRoot)`
      (prefix check, NOT `str_contains`). `..`, symlinks escaping the jail, and non-allowed roots are
      rejected with 403.
- [ ] Admin-gated (401/403 for non-admin). Non-existent path → 404.
- [ ] Suites + PHPStan L9 + PHPCS green.

## Tests + coverage target
- Unit: lists dirs; rejects `../` traversal; rejects symlink escape; rejects non-allowed root;
  non-admin blocked. Near-100% on new code — security paths MUST be covered.

## Docs
- CHANGELOG. `phlix-docs/admin/library-management.md` (note the picker endpoint + the root allowlist).

## Verification commands
```bash
cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && ./vendor/bin/phpunit \
  && ./vendor/bin/phpstan analyze src/ --level=9 && ./vendor/bin/phpcs --standard=PSR12 src/
```

## Done =
Endpoint lists dirs, jailed + admi

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-server/src/Server/Http/Controllers/Admin/FsBrowseController.php","content":"<?php

declare(strict_types=1);

namespace Phlix\Server\Http\Controllers\Admin;

use Phlix\Server\Http\Request;
use Phlix\Server\Http\Response;
use Throwable;

/**
 * Admin JSON API for browsing the filesystem when picking a library path
 * (Step 0.6).
 *
 *   - `GET /api/v1/admin/fs/browse?path=…` → the immediate SUBDIRECTORIES of
 *     `path`, restricted to the configured allowed roots. With no/empty `path`
 *     it returns the configured roots themselves as the top-level entry list,
 *     giving the picker a starting point.
 *
 * This is intentionally NOT a general file manager: it lists directories only
 * (never files), and supports no read/write/delete. Files within a directory
 * are excluded from the result.
 *
 * Path-traversal safety: every candidate path is canonicalised with
 * {@see realpath()} (which collapses `..` segments AND resolves symlinks) and
 * then checked against each allowed root with a trailing-slash prefix test —
 * `$real === $root || str_starts_with($real . '/', $root . '/')` — NEVER
 * `str_contains()`. Because realpath() canonicalises both `..` and symlinks,
 * this single check rejects `../` escapes and symlinks that point outside the
 * jail alike (both yield 403). This mirrors the canonical jail pattern in
 * {@see \Phlix\Server\Http\Controllers\AudiobookController::validateMediaPath()}.
 *
 * Route group is gated by {@see \Phlix\Server\Http\Middleware\AdminMiddleware}
 * (registered in {@see \Phlix\Server\Http\Routes\AdminRoutes}); non-admin
 * callers receive a JSON 401/403 from the middleware, so this controller
 * assumes it only runs for authenticated admins.
 *
 * Resident-memory rules: no `exit`/`die`, no blocking `sleep()`, no request
 * data parked in `static`/`global`. The allowed-roots list is shared/immutable
 * config da

file.content:
<?php

declare(strict_types=1);

// Filesystem roots the admin path-picker may browse (Step 0.6). The
// `GET /api/v1/admin/fs/browse` endpoint jails directory listing to these
// roots; only directories that resolve (via realpath) under one of them are
// listable. Keep this list conservative — it is the security boundary for the
// browse endpoint. (Env override is intentionally omitted to keep the boundary
// explicit and auditable.)
return [
    'browse_roots' => ['/home', '/mnt', '/media', '/data'],
];

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-server/CHANGELOG.md","content":"# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and the project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [Unreleased]

### Added

- **Server-wide settings store + admin API (step 0.5).** A DB-backed store so admin settings pages have somewhere to persist (the `config/*.php` files are boot-time / read-only). New migration `migrations/026_server_settings.sql` creates the typed key/value `server_settings` table (`id` CHAR(36) UUID PK, unique `setting_key`, text `setting_value`, `value_type` ENUM `string|int|bool|float|json`, timestamps; `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` so the migration runner can replay it idempotently). `src/Admin/SettingsRepository.php` (`Phlix\Admin`) models the runtime contract **config default → DB override → effective value**: the value baked into `config/<file>.php` is the baseline, a row in `server_settings` overrides it, and the effective value is the override when present else the default. Keys are *dotted* — the first segment names the config file and the rest walk the returned array (e.g. `hwaccel.enabled` reads `config/hwaccel.php['enabled']`, `port-forward.port_forwarding.upnp_enabled` walks two levels). Upserts use `INSERT ... title: Server Settings
description: Server-wide settings store and admin settings API
---

# Server Settings

Phlix keeps server-wide configuration in two layers: the read-only `config/*.php`
files baked in at boot, and a DB-backed **settings store** that lets an admin
override individual values at runtime. The store is exposed through an admin JSON
API so settings can be read and changed without editing files or restarting the
server.

::: tip UI coming in Phase 1.3
This page documents the **API** only. The graphical settings screens in the admin
console land in Phase 1.3 — until then, settings are read and written through the
endpoints below.
:::

## The Effective-Value Model

Every editable setting has up to three layers:

| Layer | Source | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| **Default** | the value in `config/<file>.php` | Baked in at boot, read-only. |
| **Override** | a row in the `server_settings` table | Written by `PUT /api/v1/admin/settings`. |
| **Effective** | the override when present, else the default | What the API returns and what the server uses. |

An override **survives a restart** because the database — not the process — is the
durable store. Removing an override (a future capability) would let the config-file
default win again.

### Dotted Keys

Settings are addressed with *dotted* keys. The first segment names the
`config/<file>.php` file; the remaining segments walk into the array that file
returns. For example:

- `hwaccel.enabled` → the `enabled` key of `config/hwaccel.php`.
- `port-forward.port_forwarding.upnp_enabled` → walks two levels into
  `config/port-forward.php`.

The leading file segment is restricted to `^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$`, so a crafted key
can never escape the config directory.

## Authentication

Both endpoints sit in the `/api/v1/admin` route group and are gated by the ad

## API Reference

### HTTP Endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/health` | Health check |
| GET | `/system/info` | Server information |
| POST | `/api/v1/auth/register` | User registration |
| POST | `/api/v1/auth/login` | User login |
| POST | `/api/v1/auth/refresh` | Token refresh |
| GET | `/api/v1/auth/me` | Current user profile |
| GET | `/api/v1/sessions` | List user sessions |
| DELETE | `/api/v1/sessions/{id}` | End a session |
| POST | `/api/v1/sessions/{id}/progress` | Report playback progress |
| GET | `/api/v1/sessions/{id}/progress` | Get playback state |
| GET | `/api/v1/admin/settings` | Effective server settings (config default + DB override) — admin-only |
| PUT | `/api/v1/admin/settings` | Persist server-setting overrides — admin-only |

### WebSocket Events

**Connection Events:**
- `connected` - Sent on successful connection
- `client_disconnected` - Broadcast when client disconnects

- **Admin filesystem-browse endpoint for the library path picker (step 0.6).** New `GET /api/v1/admin/fs/browse?path=…` (`src/Server/Http/Controllers/Admin/FsBrowseController.php`) lists the immediate **subdirectories** of `path` (directories only — never files; no read/write/delete) so a future "add library" UI can offer a path picker. New `config/filesystem.php` defines the `browse_roots` allow-list (default `['/home', '/mnt', '/media', '/data']`) — the security boundary the listing is jailed to (env override deliberately omitted to keep the boundary explicit/auditable). Traversal safety mirrors the canonical `AudiobookController::validateMediaPath()` jail: every candidate path is canonicalised with `realpath()` (which collapses `..` and resolves symlinks) and checked against each root with a trailing-slash prefix test (`$real === $root || str_starts_with($real . '/', $root . '/')`, **never** `str_contains`), so `..` escapes, symlinks pointing outside the jail, and non-allowed roots are all rejected with `403`. Status mapping: empty/absent `path` → `200` returning the configured roots as the entry list (`data.path`/`data.parent` = `null`); `realpath()` fails (non-existent) → `404`; resolves but not a directory → `400`; resolves outside the jail → `403`; valid dir under a root → `200` `{ success, data: { path, parent, entries:[{name,path}] } }` (entries sorted by name, `parent` only when it is itself within the jail else `null`). The route sits in the existing `/api/v1/admin` group registered in `src/Server/Http/Routes/AdminRoutes.php`, gated by `AdminMiddleware` (non-admin callers get a JSON 401/403); bound via a `factory()` in `AdminServicesProvider` that loads `browse_roots` from config (roots that do not `realpath()`-resolve are dropped at construction). **API only — the path-picker / library-management UI lands in Phase 1.1.** Verified by unit tests covering all security paths (traversal/symlink-escape/non-allowed-root → 403, 404, 400, roots-list, parent-within-jail, ctor-drops-non-resolving-root); new-code coverage 91.1% (72/79 statements) — the only uncovered lines are the defensively-unreachable `catch (Throwable)` → 500 and `scandir() === false` arms (a valid, jail-checked, readable directory cannot trip them).
- **Server-wide settings store + admin API (step 0.5).**
