## G. Reviewer (CUMULATIVE 1.1a+1.1b+1.1c) — 2026-05-27

> Read-only cumulative review across the three merged sub-steps as one feature. Traced every seam
> against the actual merged code (phlix-server master `1ea39af`, phlix-docs master `028b6c0`).

**Result: NO FINDINGS** that warrant a Fixer loop. Two documentation-only nits + two non-blocking
design carry-overs are recorded below for the handoff (none are blocking; one is a brand-new edge that
only surfaces when 1.1a+1.1b+1.1c are considered together).

### What I verified across the 1.1a ↔ 1.1b ↔ 1.1c seam (real code traced)

1. **Shape coherence end-to-end** — the SPA's `ScanJob` interface (`admin-ui/src/api/libraries.ts:74-88`)
   is FIELD-FOR-FIELD identical to `ScanJobRepository::decodeRow()` (`src/Media/Library/ScanJobRepository.php:362-379`):
   13 fields, same names, same types, same nullability (`id`/`library_id`/`type`/`status` strings;
   four `items_*` ints; `current_path`/`error`/`queued_at`/`started_at`/`completed_at` nullable string).
   `type` literal union `'scan'|'rescan'` matches the migration-027 ENUM + the repo's `ALLOWED_TYPES`
   const + the only values `LibraryController::scan/rescan` ever passes to `enqueue()`. `status` literal
   union `'queued'|'running'|'completed'|'failed'` matches the ENUM + the worker's `markCompleted`/
   `markFailed` writes + the SPA's `isTerminal()` set (`completed|failed`). No drift.

2. **`libraries.type` vs `LIBRARY_TYPES` vs `$validTypes`** — `libraries.type` ENUM (migration 001) =
   `movie|series|music|photo|video`. SPA `LIBRARY_TYPES` (`libraries.ts:36-42`) = those exact 5 values
   in the same order. `LibraryController::create()` `$validTypes` (`LibraryController.php:131`) STILL
   lists `book` (the documented 1.1c carry-over). NOT conflated with `library_scan_jobs.type` (which is
   `scan|rescan` ENUM). Correct.

3. **DI / constructor integration across all three sub-steps** —
   - `MediaServicesProvider` (`src/Common/Container/Providers/MediaServicesProvider.php`) autowires
     `LibraryManager` (line 92), `ScanJobRepository` (line 97), AND `LibraryScanWorker` (line 102) — all
     three sub-step deps coherent.
   - `Application::getLibraryController()` BOTH branches pass `ScanJobRepository` second (lines
     1689-1690 null-fallback `new \Phlix\Media\Library\ScanJobRepository($db)`; lines 1696-1697
     container `->get(ScanJobRepository::class)`). `PhlixMySQLConnection extends Workerman\MySQL\Connection`,
     so the fallback ctor is type-compatible.
   - `AdminRoutes::register()` and `AdminRoutesTest` do NOT touch `LibraryController` (the 0.5/0.6
     hand-rolled-container trap does not apply here). The single non-test construction site is
     `Application::getLibraryController()`; every test ctor (18 sites in `LibraryControllerTest.php`)
     was updated to pass the new `$scanJobs` mock.
   - `start.php` lines 177-207 spawn the managed `phlix-library-scan` Worker via
     `ContainerFactory::create($config)` inside `onWorkerStart` (post-fork), guarded with try/catch so a
     misconfigured worker cannot stop HTTP boot.

4. **Resident-memory / async rules** — no `exit`/`die`, no blocking `sleep`, no statics holding
   request state, no unbounded growing arrays across any of the three sub-steps. Worker uses
   `Workerman\Timer::add($pollSeconds, fn(): bool => $this->runOnce())` (`LibraryScanWorker.php:160`).
   Repository is request/worker-scoped via DI. SPA uses per-library `setInterval` in a `useRef`, cleared
   on terminal + on unmount (`LibrariesPage.tsx:117-198`). Stable `pushToast` destructure from
   `useToast()` (`LibrariesPage.tsx:93`) — confirmed `useToast` is consumed nowhere else in the SPA
   (`DashboardPage`, `NotFoundPage`, `App.tsx` do not use it), so the destructure fix is self-contained.

5. **Security across all seams** — all four 1.1b endpoints (`scan` 226, `rescan` 258, `scanStatus`
   293, `scanHistory` 322) call `requireAdmin` FIRST. Existing CRUD endpoints unchanged. SPA admin gate
   `useAdminGuard` + server `AdminMiddleware` unchanged. No tokens in URLs. No `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`
   anywhere in the new SPA code (`grep -rn "dangerouslySetInnerHTML" admin-ui/src` returns nothing). All
   server strings (names, paths, errors, `current_path` were it ever non-null) render as React text
   children. `path/traversal` in `fs/browse` (0.6) untouched. SQL injection: `ScanJobRepository` is
   fully parameterized — `updateProgress`/`markCompleted` dynamic SQL builds column names ONLY from the
   fixed `COUNTER_COLUMNS` allow-list (`ScanJobRepository.php:55-60`), values bound separately. Worker
   passes UUID + caller-provided strings only. Controller only passes `$params['id']` (route-validated
   `[^/]+`).

6. **Lifecycle integration — FK CASCADE vs in-flight scan + SPA poll** (the deepest cross-step trace).
   Trace: user deletes lib mid-scan via SPA → `confirmDelete` → `api.remove(id)` →
   `LibraryController::delete()` → `LibraryManager::deleteLibrary` → `DELETE FROM libraries WHERE id=?`
   → FK CASCADE wipes the in-flight `library_scan_jobs` row. Then the worker's subsequent
   `markCompleted`/`markFailed` UPDATE finds 0 rows (harmless, recorded in 1.1b cumulative review). On
   the SPA side, `loadLibraries()` refreshes the list (deleted lib gone), but does NOT show it; the
   per-library `setInterval` for the deleted id keeps ticking until its next `scanStatus(deletedId)`
   poll returns 404 → `pollOnce` catches → `stopPolling(deletedId)` clears the timer. The leak is
   bounded to AT MOST one extra poll cycle (≤2s default). Acceptable; bounded; auto-recovers. See
   carry-over below if you want a tighter eager cleanup.

7. **Regression surface — did 1.1c break anything older?**
   - **SSR / SPA route ordering:** `/admin/libraries` falls through to the SPA catch-all
     (`public/index.php:222-234`) because the SSR if-chain only handles literal `/admin/plugins[...]`
     (line 172) + `/admin/dashboard` (line 207) before the catch-all. The typed `Router` only handles
     `/api/v1/admin/*` (line 124) — it does NOT see browser path `/admin/libraries`. No conflict.
   - **Other `/admin/...` routes:** `loadLastFmRoutes()` registers `/admin/lastfm`/`/admin/lastfm/callback`/
     `/admin/lastfm/disconnect` on the LONG-RUNNING Application router (start.php), distinct from the
     PHP-fpm `public/index.php` SSR if-chain. No `/admin/libraries` collision.
   - **`useToast()` destructure fix:** `useToast` is consumed ONLY in `LibrariesPage.tsx` (grep
     confirmed); `DashboardPage`, `NotFoundPage`, `App.tsx` do not use it. No regression to other pages.
   - **`pages.test.tsx` NAV_ITEMS assertion:** only one test references `NAV_ITEMS` (grep confirmed) —
     no other test pins the old `[Dashboard]`-only shape.
   - **`App.tsx`'s new `<Route>`:** placed between `/` (Dashboard) and the `*` catch-all
     (`App.tsx:51-55`); React Router resolves `/`, `/libraries`, and 404 paths correctly under the
     `/admin` basename.

8. **Documentation drift across the docs PRs (#26 + #27)** — `docs/admin/library-management.md` carries
   BOTH the 1.1b "Scanning a Library" API section (lines 270+) AND the 1.1c "Managing libraries in the
   admin UI" section (lines 17-148). The 1.1c section references the 1.1b contract verbatim (URLs,
   verbs, response codes, `job_id` envelope, `scan-status`/`scan-history` paths). Both layers carry the
   same "coarse / lifecycle-only" warning callout. Cross-link integrity: `docs/dev/admin-spa.md` cites
   real symbols (`LibrariesApi`/`FilesystemApi`, `LIBRARY_TYPES`, `pushToast`, `useToast`,
   `DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS`, `isTerminal`). The `book` carry-over is documented consistently in:
   `admin/library-management.md:61-67` (warning callout), `dev/admin-spa.md:202-215` (dev rationale),
   `libraries.ts:30-42` (code comment), `CHANGELOG.md` Unreleased entry, and both worklogs.

### Cross-step edges recorded (acceptable carry-overs, NOT findings)

These are NOT review findings (no code change needed in this release) but the handoff should track them.

**Already-known carry-overs (re-confirmed across the cumulative diff — no NEW carry-overs surfaced):**

1. **`LibraryController::create()` `$validTypes` includes `book` while the DB ENUM does not.** A `book`
   create would 500. The SPA excludes `book` from `LIBRARY_TYPES`, so the UI cannot trigger it; an API
   client posting `book` directly still hits the 500. Documented as a carry-over in the 1.1c worklog +
   `library-management.md` + `dev/admin-spa.md` + CHANGELOG. Future fix: remove `book` from
   `$validTypes` OR add `book` to the migration-001 ENUM via a new migration.
2. **Stuck-`running` job on worker crash.** If `LibraryScanWorker` dies between `claimNext()` (→running)
   and `markCompleted/markFailed`, the row is stranded in `running` forever — there is no reaper /
   lease / `started_at` staleness sweep, and `claimNext()` only picks `queued` rows. A stranded job does
   NOT block new jobs (a fresh enqueue makes a new `queued` row), so acceptable now; future improvement
   is a `started_at < NOW() - INTERVAL` requeue sweep.
3. **Worker emits NO `items_*` counts.** `scanLibrary`/`rescanLibrary` return `void`; the SPA shows
   lifecycle only. Honestly documented at every layer. Future step can wire real counts through
   `ScanJobRepository::updateProgress()`.
4. **FK `ON DELETE CASCADE` race on delete-mid-scan.** Deleting a library mid-scan purges its job row;
   `markCompleted`/`markFailed` then UPDATE 0 rows — harmless no-op, no crash. Documented in 1.1b
   cumulative review.

**New edge that surfaces only at the 1.1a+1.1b+1.1c integration level (still NOT a finding — bounded
and auto-recovering):**

5. **SPA leaks one extra poll cycle (≤2s) on `confirmDelete()`.** `LibrariesPage.confirmDelete()`
   (`LibrariesPage.tsx:249-264`) does NOT call `stopPolling(deleting.id)` before/after `api.remove()`.
   The per-library `setInterval` in `timersRef.current[deletedId]` keeps firing until its next
   `scanStatus(deletedId)` request hits 404 (library gone), at which point `pollOnce`'s catch handler
   invokes `stopPolling(deletedId)` and the timer clears. So the leak is bounded to AT MOST one poll
   tick after delete (default 2000ms) and auto-recovers. **Suggested future fix** (one-line): add
   `stopPolling(deleting.id);` at the top of `confirmDelete()` (before `api.remove`) so the timer is
   torn down eagerly, eliminating the bounded leak + the wasted 404 request. Not blocking for this
   release.

### Documentation nit (non-blocking, cosmetic)

- `phlix-docs/docs/reference/api.md:233-246` — the `scan-history` example response shows only 5 fields
  (`id`, `type`, `status`, `queued_at`, `completed_at`) while the actual `decodeRow()` always returns
  ALL 13 fields. The accompanying prose ("Each entry has the same shape as the `scan_status` job row")
  refers readers to the full shape just above, so this is an abbreviated illustrative example, not a
  contract drift. Could be cleaner by either repeating the full 13-field shape or explicitly noting
  "(only common fields shown; see scan-status for the full shape)".

### Final assessment

End-to-end producer → queue → worker → readers → SPA shape, lifecycle, async-safety, security,
DI, and routing integrate coherently across all three sub-steps. No breaking changes to older callers.
No new SQLi/XSS/path-traversal/auth surface. The four documented carry-overs are honestly tracked at
every layer (controller code, SPA code, docs, CHANGELOG, worklogs). One brand-new minor edge (the
bounded SPA poll-after-delete) is documented above with a one-line suggested fix; it is auto-recovering
and not blocking.

**Status:** NO FINDINGS that require a Fixer loop. 1.1 (split into 1.1a/b/c) is complete.
WORKLOG_EOF

>   green). Spec+worklog: `steps/1.1c-*.md`.
>   - **NEW ARCHITECTURE NOTE FROM 1.1c (applies to all future SPA pages):** `ToastProvider`'s
>     context value is a `useMemo` over `[toasts, push, dismiss]`, so the WHOLE `useToast()` object is
>     a fresh reference on every toast push. Depending on it inside a `useCallback`/`useEffect` causes
>     a **feedback loop**: every toast re-fires the effect (in 1.1c this re-issued `api.list()` and
>     consumed the next-ordered fetch response as if it were the libraries list, crashing DataTable).
>     **Rule:** destructure the stable `push` callback (`const { push: pushToast } = useToast();`) and
>     depend on `push`, NOT the whole context value. The provider's `push` is itself a `useCallback`
>     over stable deps, so its reference is stable across renders. Same caveat applies to any other
>     `useMemo`'d context value the SPA introduces.
>   - **NEW TEST GOTCHA FROM 1.1c (also broadly applicable):** `<Field required>` renders the label as
>     `"Name *"` (the required asterisk is inside an `aria-hidden` span, but RTL's `getByLabelText`
>     uses the label's textContent for matching). So `screen.getByLabelText('Name')` does NOT match a
>     required field — use `getByLabelText(/^Name/)` (or `'Name *'`). Bit anyone writing tests for
>     Modal/Form fields.
> - **CUMULATIVE review 1.1a+1.1b+1.1c — NO FINDINGS** (2026-05-27): producer→queue→worker→
>   reader→SPA verified end-to-end. `ScanJob` shape field-for-field correct vs `decodeRow()` (13
>   fields); wire enums coherent across migration↔repo↔controller↔SPA; DI wiring sound in both
>   `getLibraryController()` branches; `AdminRoutes` untouched (LibraryController is in
>   `Application::loadLibraryRoutes()`, not `AdminRoutes`); all four 1.1b endpoints `requireAdmin`-gated;
>   no resident-memory violations; docs consistent across `library-management.md` + `admin-spa.md` +
>   CHANGELOG. ONE minor non-blocking edge surfaced at the cumulative seam: `LibrariesPage.confirmDelete()`
>   doesn't call `stopPolling(deleting.id)`, so a deleted-mid-scan library leaks ≤1 poll tick (≤2s)
>   before `pollOnce`'s 404 catch self-clears the interval — auto-recovers, bounded; future one-liner.
> - **CARRY-OVERS FROM THE 1.1 SPLIT** (consolidated for future steps):
>   1. **(1.1b)** Stuck-`running` job has no reaper/lease — a worker crash mid-scan strands the row
>      forever. Acceptable now (`count:1`, doesn't block new jobs). Future: add a `started_at < NOW() -
>      INTERVAL` requeue/timeout sweep.
>   2. **(1.1c — NEW backend bug uncovered)** `LibraryController::create()` `$validTypes` includes
>      `'book'`, but the `libraries.type` ENUM (migration 001) does NOT — a `book` create would 500 at
>      the DB ENUM. The SPA defends by excluding `book` from `LIBRARY_TYPES`, but the controller is
>      still wrong. **Future fix:** either drop `book` from `$validTypes` OR add `book` to the ENUM
>      via a new migration (decide before 1.2 surfaces it again).
>   3. **(1.1c)** No per-file scan progress — `scanLibrary`/`rescanLibrary` return void, so
>      `items_*` stay 0 and `current_path` is `null` in this release. The SPA shows lifecycle only.
>      Future: add counts to the worker/scanner.
>   4. **(1.1c minor)** `LibrariesPage.confirmDelete()` could `stopPolling(deleting.id)` to avoid the
>      ≤1-tick orphan poll on delete-mid-scan. Auto-recovers; one-line future improvement.

    echo

  fi
done

You are a STRICTLY READ-ONLY deep-evaluation agent. DO NOT Edit/Write/commit or mutate ANYTHING. Only read, run read-only analysis commands, and report. Do NOT run `caliber refresh`. Always `cd /home/sites/phlix/<repo> && <cmd>` in the same command (cwd is not sticky).

CONTEXT: A plan at /home/sites/phlix/plan_posters.md was partially implemented. Your job is to deeply evaluate WORKSTREAM A (WS-A) — the Continue-Watching poster + progress fix in phlix-server. The user believes the implementation may be partial or poor. Be skeptical and thorough.

WHAT WS-A WAS SUPPOSED TO DO (from the plan):
Fix `GET /api/v1/users/me/continue-watching` which returned raw DB rows instead of MediaItemShaper-shaped rows, so the `/app` SPA MediaCard showed no poster and no progress bar.
- Primary fix point: `src/Session/PlaybackController.php` getContinueWatching() (~lines 348-387).
- Reuse `src/Media/Library/MediaItemShaper.php` shape().
- Endpoint wrapper: `src/Server/WebPortal/WebPortalRouter.php:1565-1584` filters via `$gate->filterItems($items, $filter, 'media_item_id')` — so `media_item_id` MUST survive the reshape.
Design (must ALL be present):
  A2: `LEFT JOIN media_items p ON mi.parent_id = p.id`; select parent metadata; for episode rows resolve a real poster (episode own poster only if genuine → season → parent SERIES poster) into metadata.poster_url BEFORE shaping; movies keep own poster. Preserve ROW_NUMBER dedup + LIMIT ?.
  A3: run each row through MediaItemShaper::shape() → top-level `id` (= MEDIA ITEM id, not playback_state id), `poster_url`, `runtime`, `poster_srcset`, `year`, `rating`. Re-attach top-level `position_ticks` + `duration_ticks`. Preserve `media_item_id` + `metadata` map.
  A4: JSON row must satisfy MediaCard.vue (top-level poster_url, runtime in MINUTES, id=media item id) and useResumeSync.ts (reads top-level position_ticks). No Smarty edits.
Acceptance: phpstan + phpunit green; a test asserts an episode CW row yields top-level series poster_url, top-level runtime, id=media item id, and retained position_ticks/duration_ticks/media_item_id/metadata. Prefer a REAL-MySQL integration test for the JOIN (mock-DB tests have hidden real query bugs before).

RELEVANT COMMITS (phlix-server): 2beaa0a6 (main fix), 19b2196f (test mock fields), 7d5a40a8 (return type). But verify against the ACTUAL current code, not just commit messages.

YOUR EVALUATION TASKS:
1. Read the current `PlaybackController::getContinueWatching()` in full. Verify each of A2/A3/A4 design points is actually present and CORRECT. Specifically check: (a) is there a LEFT JOIN to parent media_items? (b) is episode→series poster resolution actually done, and is the ordering correct (does it avoid using the still)? (c) is MediaItemShaper::shape() actually called per row? (d) is top-level `id` the MEDIA ITEM id or accidentally still the playback_state id? (e) are position_ticks + duration_ticks re-attached at top level AFTER shaping (shaping could strip them)? (f) are media_item_id and metadata preserved so the gate filter and console still work?
2. Check `WebPortalRouter.php` around 1565-1584: does `filterItems(..., 'media_item_id')` still find media_item_id after reshape? Could the shaping have broken the gate filter?
3. Check MediaItemShaper::shape() — does it actually emit runtime in the shape the SPA expects (minutes)? Does it require input columns that the CW query does NOT select (→ would silently drop poster_url/runtime)?
4. Tests: find the test(s) for getContinueWatching. Do they actually assert the acceptance criteria (series poster for episode, runtime>0, id=media item id, retained ticks/media_item_id/metadata)? Or are they weak/mock-only? Is there a real-MySQL integration test as the plan requested? Note commit 19b2196f "add missing mock data fields to fix PHP warnings" — investigate whether the test is papering over real issues.
5. Run static analysis + tests SCOPED to this area to reduce load:
   - `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/Session/PlaybackController.php 2>&1 | tail -40` (confirm phpstan config; if it needs `-c phpstan.neon.dist` use that)
   - `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpcs src/Session/PlaybackController.php 2>&1 | tail -40`
   - Find and run the relevant phpunit test class only, e.g. `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpunit --filter ContinueWatching 2>&1 | tail -40` (adjust filter to the real test name).
   - Paste ACTUAL output.
6. Cross-step regression check: did any later step (e.g. D-SRV searchMedia/getMusicTracks additions in commit 3cc9ba34, or the return-type commit) move or break code that A relied on?

REPORT FORMAT (return as your final message — it is data for the parent, not a human chat):
- Per sub-step A2/A3/A4/tests: verdict = DONE | PARTIAL | NOT_DONE | BROKEN, with concrete file:line evidence and exactly what is missing/wrong.
- A "Problems" list, each with: severity (blocker/major/minor), file:line, precise description, and suggested fix direction.
- Actual phpstan/phpcs/phpunit output (trimmed to relevant lines).
- Bottom line: is WS-A correctly and fully done? If not, what remains?

| **C** | Logging triple-write fix (app/events/plugins get identical copies) | phlix-server | Bug fix |
| **D** | Retire the legacy **Smarty** UI — **migrate every Smarty-only surface to `/app` first, then delete Smarty** | phlix-server, phlix-hub, phlix-ui | Migration + cleanup |

  `home/index.tpl` + `partials/media_card.t

/**
 * Shapes a raw hydrated media-item DB row into the public `media-item.schema.json`
 * response format (poster URLs, genres, overview, year, the season/episode
 * hierarchy fields, …).
 *
 * Extracted so EVERY endpoint that returns a media item produces the SAME shape.
 * The list endpoint (`GET /api/v1/media`) already enriched its rows, but the
 * single-item endpoint (`GET /api/v1/media/{id}`) used by the detail and player
 * pages returned the raw row — so posters, overview, genres and the season/
 * episode numbers were all absent and those pages rendered blank. Both paths now
 * call this shaper.
 */
final class MediaItemShaper
{
    /** Media-item `type` enum (schema-constrained). */
    private const VALID_TYPES = ['movie', 'series', 'season', 'episode', 'audio', 'image'];

    /**
     * Content-rating enum (schema-constrained). The MPAA movie ratings PLUS the
     * US TV ratings, interleaved on the shared restrictiveness scale (see
     * {@see ContentRating}). `NR` is normalized to `UNRATED` and so is absent.
     *
     * @var list<string>
     */
    private const VALID_RATINGS = [
        'G',
        'TV-Y',
        'TV-G',
        'TV-Y7',
        'PG',
        'TV-PG',
        'PG-13',
        'TV-14',
        'R',
        'TV-MA',
        'NC-17',
        'X',
        'UNRATED',

WHAT WS-C WAS SUPPOSED TO DO:
Symptom: every log message written to app.log, events.log AND plugins.log identically (triple firehose); only error.log correctly filtered. Root cause: `src/Common/Logger/StructuredLogger.php` setupHandlers() (~78-96) pushes EVERY handler from config/logger.php onto EVERY channel's logger with no channel→handler routing. /**

 *

declare(strict_types=1);

namespace Phlix\Common\Logger;

use Monolog\Handler\HandlerInterface;
use Monolog\Handler\RotatingFileHandler;
use Monolog\Handler\StreamHandler;
use Monolog\Level;
use Monolog\Logger;
use Monolog\LogRecord;
use Monolog\Processor\PsrLogMessageProcessor;
use Psr\Log\LoggerInterface;
use Stringable;

/**
 * Phlix wrapper around Monolog 3 that exposes a PSR-3 surface while
 * also accepting Monolog {@see Level} objects directly.
 *
 * Config shape (`array<string, mixed>`):
 * ```
 * [
 *     'handlers' => [
 *         '<name>' => [
 *             'type' => 'rotating_file'|'stream'|'error'|'audit',
 *             'path' => string,
 *             'level' => string, // optional, defaults to 'debug'
 *             'max_files' => int, // optional
 *             'channels' => string[], // optional; channels this handler
 *                                     // serves. ABSENT/empty = all channels.
 *             'env' => string, // optional; name of an env var that must be
 *                              // truthy for the handler to attach.
 *         ],
 *         ...
 *     ],
 *     'processors' => [
 *         'request_id' => bool,
 *         'user_id'    => bool,
 *     ],
 * ]
 * ```
 *
 * Handler routing: a handler attaches to this logger only when its
 * `channels` gate admits {@see $channel} AND its `env` gate is satisfied.
 * This keeps subsystem logs (events.log, plugins.log) scoped to their own
 * channels instead of receiving every application record, and avoids the
 * write-amplification of pushing all handlers onto every channel.
 *
 * @package Phlix\Common\Logger
 */
class StructuredLogger implements LoggerInterface\

use Phlix\Common\Logger\LogChannels;

/*
 * Handler routing (see StructuredLogger::setupHandlers()):
 *
 * Each handler may declare two optional gates that decide whether it
 * attaches to a given per-channel logger:
 *   - `channels`: list of channel names this handler serves. ABSENT (or an
 *     empty list) = attach to ALL channels.
 *   - `env`: name of an environment variable that must be truthy
 *     (1/true/yes/on) for the handler to attach. ABSENT = always attach.
 *
 * Without these gates every handler attaches to every channel, which is
 * what caused each debug/info record to be written to app.log + events.log
 * + plugins.log (3x write-amplification) and polluted the two subsystem
 * logs with unrelated application records (item-5b).
 *
 * Resulting routing:
 *   - app.log     — ALL records from ALL channels (the general app log;
 *                   capturing everything here is intended, so no diagnostic
 *                   coverage is lost).
 *   - error.log   — ALL error-and-above records from ALL channels (error
 *                   aggregation across the whole app).
 *   - events.log  — ONLY the EVENTS channel, and ONLY when
 *                   PHLIX_DEBUG_EVENTS is truthy; otherwise stays empty.
 *   - plugins.log — ONLY the PLUGINS channel (plugin lifecycle).
 */
return [
    'default' => 'file',
    'handlers' => [
        // General application log — every channel, every level (debug+).
        'file' => [
            'type' => 'rotating_file',
            'path' => __DIR__ . '/../.logs/app.log',
            'max_files' => 30,
            'level' => 'debug',
        ],
        // Error aggregation — every channel, error level and above.
        'error' => [
            'type' => 'rotating_file',
            'path' => __DIR__ . '/../.logs/error.log',
            'max_files' => 30,
            'le

> **How to use this file:** Point a fresh session (opencode or Claude) at this file and say
> *"Read `/home/sites/phlix/plan_posters.md` and follow it."* It is fully self-contained — it carries the
> root-cause analysis, exact files/lines, the orchestration model, git/commit rules, opencode-specific notes,
> and the full UI rebuild/release runbook. Do not re-investigate before starting; the findings below were
> produced by dedicated investigation agents against the live code (2026-07-18).
>
> **Scope note:** this started as a poster bug but now covers four workstreams (A–D). The filename stays
> `plan_posters.md` for continuity.

---

## 0. TL;DR — the four workstreams

| WS | Title | Repos | Nature |
|----|-------|-------|--------|
| **A** | Continue-Watching poster + progress fix (the `/app` SPA bug) | phlix-server (data layer) | Bug fix |
| **B** | Console-client poster + detail-image fixes | phlix-console-client | Bug fix |
| **C** | Logging triple-write fix (app/events/plugins get identical copies) | phlix-server | Bug fix |
| **D** | Retire the legacy **Smarty** UI — **migrate every Smarty-only surface to `/app` first, then delete Smarty** | phlix-server, phlix-hub, phlix-ui | Migration + cleanup |

### Critical correction confirmed by investigation (read before anything else)

The **server-rendered Smarty interface is DEAD LEGACY** in both phlix-server and phlix-hub. In both, the root
path `/` **redirects to the `/app` SPA** (`@phlix/ui`, the Vue 3 app), and the Smarty home page is unreachable:

- phlix-server: `public/index.php:263-266` and `src/Server/Workerman/HttpHandler.php:1320-1324` both redirect

Do NOT trust CHANGELOG entries, commit messages, or worklogs. A CHANGELOG claiming "Books page migrated" proves nothing; the .vue page, its route, and the server API it calls must actually exist and be wired. Confirm by reading files.

CONTEXT: Plan /home/sites/phlix/plan_posters.md partially implemented. Evaluate WORKSTREAM D, Phase D-SRV — retiring the legacy Smarty UI in phlix-server: first MIGRATE any in-use Smarty-only media reader surfaces to the `/app` Vue SPA (phlix-ui / @phlix/ui), verify parity, THEN delete the Smarty stack (EXCEPT the newsletter email path, which must be KEPT). Repos: /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server and /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui. Be skeptical; user thinks work is partial/poor.

SURFACES IN QUESTION (D-SRV-0 audit → migrate if in use → D-SRV-DEL delete):
- Books (browse/detail/reader): Smarty books/{books,book,reader}.tpl — no dedicated SPA pages before. Migrate if in use.
- Audiobooks (browse/detail/player): Smarty audiobooks/{audiobooks,audiobook,player}.tpl. Migrate if in use.
- Photo (albums/album/photo/slideshow/EXIF): Smarty photo/*.tpl. Migrate if in use.
- WebAuthn passkey settings: Smarty auth/webauthn-settings.tpl (/settings/security); SettingsPage.vue exists but passkey screen unconfirmed. Migrate if missing.
- Newsletter email: emails/newsletter.tpl — NOT a UI page; server email gen via src/Admin/NewsletterGenerator.php (use Smarty). MUST BE KEPT. So smarty/smarty composer dep MUST be RETAINED on the server.

D-SRV-DEL was to delete: public/templates/{home,library,auth,search,settings,layouts,partials,music,books,audiobooks,photo}/ and admin/{dashboard.tpl,lastfm.tpl,layout.tpl,music,plugins}/ — but KEEP public/templates/emails/newsletter.tpl; src/Server/WebPortal/PageRenderer.php page methods; page controllers Music/Book/Audiobook/Photo/PluginAdminPageController.php; SSR branches in HttpHandler::dispatch() (~1326-1373) + helpers + parallel branches in public/index.php; Smarty render path in LastfmController.php:146 (keep JSON/OAuth); ThemeMiddleware IFF only post-processes Smarty HTML; templates_c cache dirs. KEEP: SharedUiController+ViteAssets, WebPortalRouter (JSON /api/v1/*), `/`→`/app` redirect + /app branch, AND smarty/smarty + newsletter.tpl (NewsletterGenerator needs it).

RELEVANT COMMITS: 3cc9ba34 (add searchMedia + getMusicTracks API endpoints, D-SRV), e75291f (@phlix/ui v0.84.0 D-SRV surfaces), 5c1726d3 (bump @phlix/ui v0.84.0 rebuild), c5e3549 (phlix-ui dist v0.84.0: "search, security, music sub-pages, books, audiobooks, photos"). NOTE: I see NO commit deleting the server Smarty stack — investigate whether D-SRV-DEL happened at all, or only page-adding happened.

   - `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && grep -rn "audiobook\|'book'\|\"book\"\|photo" src/Media/Library 2>/dev/null | head -30`
2. For EACH surface (Books, Audiobooks, Photo, WebAuthn/passkeys): does a real Vue page exist in phlix-ui SOURCE (not dist)? Is it routed? Does it call a real server API that EXISTS?
   - `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && ls src/pages 2>/dev/null | grep -i "book\|audio\|photo\|security\|search\|music"; grep -rn "books\|audiobooks\|photo\|security\|passkey\|webauthn" src/app 2>/dev/null | head -50`
   - Confirm the backing server endpoints exist: searchMedia, getMusicTracks, plus any books/audiobooks/photo/webauthn APIs the pages call. `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && grep -rn "searchMedia\|getMusicTracks\|webauthn\|passkey\|/books\|/audiobooks\|/photo" src/Server/WebPortal/WebPortalRouter.php 2>/dev/null | head -40`
   - If a page calls an API route that does NOT exist on the server → BROKEN. Verify both ends.
3. DELETION STATUS (D-SRV-DEL): Is the server Smarty stack still present? Check:
   - `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && ls public/templates/ 2>/dev/null; ls src/Server/WebPortal/PageRenderer.php src/Server/WebPortal/Controllers/*PageController.php 2>/dev/null; grep -n "smarty/smarty" composer.json`
   - Report clearly whether deletion happened, partially happened, or not at all.
4. IF Smarty deletion DID happen anywhere: CRITICAL regression check — is NewsletterGenerator.php + emails/newsletter.tpl + smarty/smarty dep still intact? (Deleting them would break newsletter email.) `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && ls public/templates/emails/newsletter.tpl 2>/dev/null; grep -n "use Smarty\|new Smarty\|Smarty(" src/Admin/NewsletterGenerator.php 2>/dev/null`
5. Cross-step regression: the D-SRV additions (searchMedia/getMusicTracks in commit 3cc9ba34) touched WebPortalRouter — did they break/move anything WS-A relied on (getContinueWatching wrapper at ~1565-1584, filterItems media_item_id)? Read that region.
6. Verification (read-only; scope narrowly, skip heavy full builds and note if skipped):
   - `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/Server/WebPortal/WebPortalRouter.php 2>&1 | tail -40` (confirm config, try `-c phpstan.neon.dist`)
   - phlix-ui typecheck if fast: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && npx vue-tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | tail -40` (else skip, note)
   - Any relevant phpunit: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpunit --filter "Search\|Music\|Book\|Audiobook\|Photo" 2>&1 | tail -40`

REPORT FORMAT (final message = structured data):
- Table: each surface (Books/Audiobooks/Photo/WebAuthn) → in use? migrated? (page file:line, route wired?, backing API exists?), verdict DONE|PARTIAL|NOT_DONE|MISSING|BROKEN.
- Deletion status: did D-SRV-DEL happen? What remains? Is newsletter/Smarty-dep correctly KEPT?
- Cross-step regressions found.
- Problems list: severity, file:line, description, suggested fix.
- Verification output (trimmed).
- Bottom line: is D-SRV fully & correctly done (audit + migrate in-use + delete-except-newsletter)? What remains?

### A2 — Parent-series JOIN + poster resolution — verdict: DONE (minor robustness caveat)
- `PlaybackController.php:391-392` adds **two** LEFT JOINs: `LEFT JOIN media_items p ON mi.parent_id = p.id` (season) and `LEFT JOIN media_items s ON p.parent_id = s.id` (series). Selects `p.metadata_json AS parent_metadata_json` and `s.metadata_json AS series_metadata_json` (`:382-383`). Better than the plan's single JOIN — it resolves the true grandparent series poster, not just the immediate parent.
- ROW_NUMBER dedup (`PARTITION BY ps.media_item_id ORDER BY ps.updated_at DESC, ps.id DESC`) + outer `WHERE ranked.rn = 1 ... LIMIT ?` preserved (`:384-400`).
- Poster resolution ordering (`:413-444`): series poster → season poster → keep episode still, gated by a `hasRealOwnPoster` heuristic. {"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/steps/item-5b.worklog.md","content":"# item-5b — Logger channel/env-gated handler attachment

Fix the logger's unconditional multi-handler attachment (3x write-amplification +
events/plugins log pollution) and implement the `PHLIX_DEBUG_EVENTS` gate the config claimed.

## Implementer — 2026-07-18

### Problem confirmed
`StructuredLogger::setupHandlers()` pushed EVERY handler in `config/logger.php` onto EVERY
channel (`LoggerFactory::get()` loads the same config for all channels). So every debug/info
record hit app.log + events.log + plugins.log (3x), and events.log/plugins.log were polluted
with unrelated app records. The config's `PHLIX_DEBUG_EVENTS` promise for events.log was never
implemented in the logger stack (only in `EventDispatcherFactory` for the PSR-14 decorator).

### Changes
- `config/logger.php` — added optional per-handler gates: `channels` (list; ABSENT/empty = all
  channels) and `env` (env var name that must be truthy; ABSENT = always). `events` →
  `channels: [LogChannels::EVENTS], env: LogChannels::DEBUG_EVENTS_ENV`; `plugins` →
  `channels: [LogChannels::PLUGINS]`. `file`/`error` left unrestricted (all channels).
  Rewrote comments to describe the routing. No change to any level/path/formatter.
- `src/Common/Logger/LogChannels.php` — added `DEBUG_EVENTS_ENV = 'PHLIX_DEBUG_EVENTS'` as the
  symbolic source shared by config + logger (mirrors EventDispatcherFactory::DEBUG_ENV_VAR;
  that file was NOT touched).
- `src/Common/Logger/StructuredLogger.php` — `setupHandlers()` now skips a handler unless
  `handlerAppliesToChannel()` (no `channels` OR `$this->channel` ∈ list) AND
  `handlerEnvEnabled()` (no `env` OR `getenv(env)` ∈ {1,true,yes,on}, case-insensitive — same
  interpretation as `EventDispatcherFactory::debugEnabled()`). Levels/formatters/processors
  unchanged. Updated class docblock config-shape.
- `tests/Unit/Common/Logger/StructuredLoggerRoutingTest.php` —

## general-purpose (WS-C read-only evaluation) — plan_posters.md §5 — 2026-07-18

Verdict: WS-C symptom (triple-write firehose) is GENUINELY FIXED, but the implementation
DIVERGES from the plan's literal C2/C3/C4 design in two documented ways.

- C2 config tags: DONE-functional / DIVERGENT-vs-letter. events→channels+env, plugins→channels.
  error/file left with NO `channels` key (= attach-to-all). `'default'=>'file'` (config line 31)
  is INERT — setupHandlers never reads it (grep: no `config['default']` access). Plan wanted
  file marked as default/catch-all-only-when-no-dedicated-handler; instead file is a full firehose.
- C3 routing: DONE-real / DIVERGENT-semantics. handlerAppliesToChannel + handlerEnvEnabled give
  real routing; but file attaches to ALL channels (not "only when no dedicated handler matches").
  Extra PHLIX_DEBUG_EVENTS env-gate added on events.log (not in plan).
- C4 tests: DONE-strong / OPPOSITE-of-plan-letter. Tests assert app.log CONTAINS plugins/events/
  media records (catch-all), whereas plan §5 C4 wanted app.log EMPTY for plugins. Tests DO assert
  events/plugins emptiness for non-matching channels + mis-scope mutation guard + error-agg + env
  truthiness. 22 tests / 65 assertions green. phpstan -l9 clean. phpcs PSR12 clean on WS-C files.
- Live disk: events-2026-07-18.log == plugins-2026-07-18.log BYTE-IDENTICAL (21241067 B), both
  FROZEN at 10:38 (pre-fix firehose era); app.log grew to 25MB/17:29. Confirms fix deployed:
  events/plugins no longer receive the firehose.

Bottom line: real bug fixed; app.log intentionally kept as full firehose (documented "no coverage
loss"); events.log env-gated OFF by default. Not "broken", but does not meet plan's literal C4.
EOF
echo done

# WS-B Evaluation — phlix-console-client poster rails + detail/search images

## Bottom line
**WS-B production code is DONE and correct for B2/B3/B4/B5. The TEST acceptance is NOT met** — the four tests that would prove the core `resolveUrl()` "relative→absolute" behavior are **skipped** (SearchScreen), and the Browse/Detail "relative URL" tests that *do* run are **false-confidence** (they assert the OLD "relative dropped" semantics and pass only by coincidence). Separately, WS-B commit `01e8c31` left **two RED tests** exposing a real "resolve-before-scheme-check" bug in the Photos screens (same bug class the plan warned about — just not in the Browse/Detail/Search files).

---

## Per sub-step verdicts

### B2 (rails show stills → topLevel=1) — **DONE**
- `src/Api/MediaQuery.php:46-49` `forLibrary()` passes `topLevel: true`; `toParams():98-100` emits `topLevel => '1'` only when `=== true`.
- Drill-in guard is structurally correct: `DetailScreen.php:297` and `:351` build child queries as `new MediaQuery(parentId: …)` with **no** topLevel; `forLibrary()` never sets `parentId`. Rails=topLevel, drill-in=parentId, never both.
- Call site `BrowseScreen.php:197` uses `MediaQuery::forLibrary(...)`. Correct.

### B3 (detail page image) — **DONE**
- `DetailScreen.php`: baseUrl ctor param at `:111`; `resolveUrl()` at `:337-344`; applied **before** the scheme chec

tool_input.prompt:
You are an implementer+fixer working in repo /home/sites/phlix/phlix-console-client (PHP 8.3, ReactPHP + SugarCraft TUI). Rules: always `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-console-client && <cmd>` in the same command (cwd is not sticky). Do NOT run `caliber refresh`. Do NOT open PRs. Commit directly to master. Do NOT edit any Smarty templates. This repo uses phpstan (config `phpstan.neon`) and phpunit; there is NO phpcs.

CONTEXT: WS-B of plan_posters.md fixed console poster rails/detail/search. A deep evaluation found the production code correct BUT a real bug + broken test coverage. Fix ALL of the following. Verify against the ACTUAL code (read the files first). Do NOT just trust this description — confirm line numbers before editing since they may have shifted.

TASKS (fix every one):

1. [HIGH — real bug] `src/Screen/PhotoAlbumScreen.php` (~line 210) and `src/Screen/PhotosScreen.php` (~line 262): the URL scheme check (`parse_url($card->posterUrl, PHP_URL_SCHEME)`) runs on the RAW posterUrl BEFORE `resolveUrl()` is applied (resolveUrl currently only applied later, ~:224 / ~:276 inside loadCover()). This drops all RELATIVE thumbnails. Fix: resolve FIRST, then scheme-check, mirroring the correct pattern in `src/Screen/DetailScreen.php` (~:440-451): 
   `$url = $this->resolveUrl($card->posterUrl); if ($url === '') { continue/skip; } $scheme = parse_url($url, PHP_URL_SCHEME); ...` and pass the RESOLVED $url onward to the loader.
   Confirm both screens have a `resolveUrl()` + `baseUrl`; if a screen lacks baseUrl wiring, check how it's constructed (grep App.php) and wire `$this->api->baseUrl()` consistently with DetailScreen. There are 2 RED tests that must go green:
   - `tests/Screen/PhotoAlbumScreenTest.php::testInitResolvesARelativeThumbnailAgainstTheBase`
   - `tests/Screen/PhotosScreenTest.php::testAlbumCoverResolvesARelativeThumbnailAgainstTheBase`

2. [HIGH — acceptance] `tests/Screen/SearchScreenTest.php`: 4 tests were `markTestSkipped` by commit 6003673 (reason "inconsistent behavior in test context"): the poster-URL tests around lines 295/327/356/386 (empty-skip, relative-resolved-and-loaded, malformed-skip, non-http-skip). The skip masks a TEST-HARNESS flaw (they call runBatch on load closures against fake hosts that don't resolve, so counts are always 0), NOT a production bug — production resolveUrl in SearchScreen is correct. UNSKIP and REPAIR them using the proven real-local-server pattern already used by the Photos tests (`tests/Screen/PhotoAlbumScreenTest.php` / `PhotosScreenTest.php` — read them to copy the local HTTP server setup), OR by asserting the scheduled batch children / resolved URL rather than counting executed loads. The key assertion that must genuinely pass: a RELATIVE posterUrl is resolved to `baseUrl + path` and a load is scheduled for the ABSOLUTE url; empty/malformed/non-http are skipped.

3. [MEDIUM — false-confidence tests] Rewrite these vacuous tests so they actually exercise URL resolution (they currently assert the PRE-FIX "relative dropped" semantics and pass for the wrong reason):
   - `tests/Screen/DetailScreenTest.php` around :519, :543, :562, :580 (child poster empty/relative/malformed/non-http variants — the `Down` key doesn't move a 2-item grid so they're vacuous). Make them assert that a relative child poster is resolved to an absolute URL and a load is scheduled (empty/malformed/non-http skipped).
   - `tests/Screen/BrowseScreenTest.php` around :749-785 (relative/malformed/non-http assert "no load" but a load IS scheduled with baseUrl). Assert the resolved-absolute URL load is scheduled.
   Use the same local-server or scheduled-children inspection technique so they pass for the RIGHT reason.

4. [LOW] `tests/Api/MediaQueryTest.php::testForLibrary` (~:17): add an assertion `self::assertSame('1', $params['topLevel'])` (adjust to the actual params accessor) — proving forLibrary emits topLevel=1.

DO NOT attempt to fix the 12 `PlayerScreenTest` failures — they are pre-existing and unrelated to WS-B (FakeTransport response-ordering). Leave them alone; note them in your report.

VERIFICATION (run and paste ACTUAL output):
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-console-client && vendor/bin/phpstan analyse 2>&1 | tail -20`
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-console-client && vendor/bin/phpunit tests/Screen/PhotoAlbumScreenTest.php tests/Screen/PhotosScreenTest.php tests/Screen/SearchScreenTest.php tests/Screen/DetailScreenTest.php tests/Screen/BrowseScreenTest.php tests/Api/MediaQueryTest.php 2>&1 | tail -30`
- Confirm the previously-RED Photos tests and previously-SKIPPED Search tests now PASS (0 skipped among the WS-B tests, 0 failures in these files).

COMMIT: when green, `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-console-client && git add -A && git commit -m "fix(ws-b): resolve poster URL before scheme check in Photos screens; repair skipped/vacuous resolveUrl tests" && git push origin master`. Then report: exactly what you changed (file:line), the actual phpstan+phpunit output, confirmation of the commit hash (`git log --oneline -1`), and any remaining concerns. Report is DATA for the parent, not a chat message.

CONTEXT: WS-A of plan_posters.md fixed Continue-Watching row shaping in `src/Session/PlaybackController.php::getContinueWatching()`. The production code is functionally correct (two-level LEFT JOIN season+series, series-poster resolution for episodes, MediaItemShaper::shape() per row, top-level id=media_item id, re-attached position_ticks/duration_ticks/media_item_id/metadata). A deep eval found the TESTS don't cover the actual /app bug and there are minor code gaps. Fix ALL of the following. READ the current code first; line numbers may have shifted — confirm before editing.

TASKS:

1. [MAJOR — test gap] `tests/Unit/Session/PlaybackControllerTest.php`: existing tests assert `result[0]['metadata']['poster_url']` (console path) but NEVER assert the TOP-LEVEL `result[0]['poster_url']` or TOP-LEVEL `result[0]['runtime']` — which are the actual `/app` MediaCard bug (SPA reads top-level poster_url and derives progress from runtime*60). Add/strengthen assertions so at least one test proves: for an EPISODE CW row → top-level `poster_url` == the resolved SERIES poster (not the still), and top-level `runtime` > 0 when the media_items row's metadata_json carries a runtime. Ensure a test row actually puts `runtime` into metadata_json so top-level runtime is exercised. Also assert top-level `id` == media item id (may already exist — keep it).

2. [MAJOR — no integration test] Add a REAL-MySQL integration test for the JOIN/dedup (the plan explicitly wanted this because mock-DB tests have hidden real query bugs before). Look at `tests/Integration/` for the existing real-MySQL test harness/base class and DB bootstrap (there are prior integration tests, e.g. metrics ONLY_FULL_GROUP_BY). Create e.g. `tests/Integration/Session/ContinueWatchingIntegrationTest.php` that: seeds `media_items` (a series, a season child, an episode grandchild with its still as poster; and a standalone movie) + `playback_state` rows, calls the real `PlaybackController::getContinueWatching()` against the live test DB, and asserts the shaped output: episode row → top-level series poster_url, top-level runtime, id=media item id, retained position_ticks/duration_ticks/media_item_id/metadata; movie row keeps its own poster. If the integration harness requires a DB that may be unavailable in this environment, follow the SAME skip/guard convention the existing integration tests use (do not invent a new one) so it self-skips cleanly when no DB — but it MUST run and pass when the DB is present. Read an existing integration test to copy the exact pattern (DB connection, table setup/teardown, skip guard).

3. [MINOR] The SQL in getContinueWatching omits `mi.parent_id`, so the mapper's `$item['parent_id'] = $row['parent_id'] ?? null` is always null. Add `mi.parent_id` to the inner AND outer SELECT and pass it through the mapper so shaped episode rows carry a real parent_id.

4. [MINOR] Still-detection currently only overrides to the series poster when `still_url` is present in metadata (TMDB path). For robustness: for `type === 'episode'`, prefer the resolved series/season poster whenever one exists, rather than only when a still is positively detected — so non-TMDB episodes (poster_url is a still but no still_url field) also get the series poster. Keep movies unchanged (movies keep own poster). Be careful not to regress the existing passing unit tests (adjust them only if the new, more-correct behavior legitimately changes an expectation — and document why in your report).

5. [MINOR] Fix the phpcs line-length warning on the `@return` docblock at `src/Session/PlaybackController.php` (~:348, 191 chars > 120). Wrap it to ≤120 per PSR12/repo convention.

VERIFICATION (paste ACTUAL output):
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/Session/PlaybackController.php 2>&1 | tail -20`
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpcs --standard=PSR12 src/Session/PlaybackController.php 2>&1 | tail -20`
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpunit --filter ContinueWatching 2>&1 | tail -30`
- If you added an integration test, run it: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpunit tests/Integration/Session/ContinueWatchingIntegrationTest.php 2>&1 | tail -30` (report whether it PASSED or self-skipped-for-no-DB, and WHY).

COMMIT when green: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && git add -A && git commit -m "test(ws-a): assert top-level poster_url/runtime + real-DB integration test; select parent_id; harden episode still-detection" && git push origin master`. Then report: exact changes (file:line), actual verification output, commit hash (`git log --oneline -1`), whether the integration test ran or skipped and why, and any concerns. <?php

declare(strict_types=1);

namespace Phlix\Tests\Integration\Session;

use Phlix\Common\Database\ConnectionPool;
use Phlix\Common\Uuid;
use Phlix\Session\PlaybackController;
use Phlix\Session\SessionManager;
use PHPUnit\Framework\TestCase;
use Throwable;
use Workerman\MySQL\Connection;

/**
 * Real-DB proof that {@see PlaybackController::getContinueWatching()} shapes the
 * continue-watching rail correctly when driven against live MySQL.
 *
 * The unit tests {@see \Phlix\Tests\Unit\Session\PlaybackControllerTest} mock the
 * connection and hand the mapper hand-built rows — so they validate the PHP
 * shaping but NOT the two-level `LEFT JOIN` (season parent + series grandparent),
 * the `ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY …)` dedup, or the `mi.parent_id` /
 * `metadata_json` columns the SQL actually projects. WS-A of plan_posters.md
 * explicitly wanted a real-DB test here because mock-DB suites have hidden real
 * query bugs before (e.g. the metrics ONLY_FULL_GROUP_BY 1055 that only surfaced
 * against MySQL — see {@see \Phlix\Tests\Integration\Stats\MetricsReadQueriesTest}).
 *
 * It seeds a full series → season → episode hierarchy (episode poster = its TMDB
 * still) plus a standalone movie, records playback progress for the episode and
 * the movie, then asserts the shaped output: the episode row surfaces the SERIES
 * poster at the TOP LEVEL (the /app MediaCard bug), a positive top-level runtime,
 * id == media item id, a real parent_id, and the retained playback fields; the
 * movie keeps its own poster. CI applies all migrations to the `phlix_test` MySQL
 * service before the suite; locally, with no reachable MySQL, it self-skips —
 * the same guard {@see \Phlix\Tests\Integration\Stats\MetricsReadQueriesTest} uses.
 *
 * @covers \Phlix\Session\PlaybackController
 */
final class ContinueWatchingIntegrationTest extends TestCase
{
    private ?Connection $db = null;

    private string $libraryId = '';
    private string $userId = '';
    private string $sessionId = '';
    private string $seriesId = '';
    private string $seasonId = '';
    private string $episodeId = '';
    private string $movieId = '';

    protected function setUp(): void
    {
        parent::setUp();

        $host = getenv('DB_HOST') ?: '127.0.0.1';
        $port = (int) (getenv('DB_PORT') ?: 3306);

        if (!$this->isMysqlReachable($host, $port)) {
            $this->markTestSkipped(
                sprintf('No MySQL on %s:%d — skipping continue-watching integration test. Runs in CI.', $host, $port),
            );
        }

        try {
            ConnectionPool::init(dirname(__DIR__, 3) . '/config/database.php');
            $this->db = ConnectionPool::getConnection('mysql');
        } catch (Throwable $e) {
            $this->markTestSkipped('Could not connect to MySQL: ' . $e->getMessage());
        }

        $this->assertNotNull($this->db);

        $this->libraryId = Uuid::v4();
        $this->userId = Uuid::v4();
        $this->sessionId = Uuid::v4();
        $this->seriesId = Uuid::v4();
        $this->seasonId = Uuid::v4();
        $this->episodeId = Uuid::v4();
        $this->movieId = Uuid::v4();

        $this->seedFixtures();
    }

    protected function tearDown(): void
    {
        $this->purgeFixtures();
        parent::tearDown();
    }

    /**
     * The episode CW row must carry the SERIES poster at the top level (the SPA
     * MediaCard reads top-level `poster_url`), a positive top-level `runtime`,
     * id == media item id, a real parent_id, and the retained playback fields.
     * The movie must keep its own poster.
     */
    public function testEpisodeRowSurfacesSeriesPosterAndRuntimeAgainstRealDb(): void
    {
        $this->assertNotNull($this->db);
        $controller = new PlaybackController($this->db, $this->createMock(SessionManager::class));

        $result = $controller->getContinueWatching($this->userId, 10);

        $episode = $this->rowFor($result, $this->episodeId);
        $movie = $this->rowFor($result, $this->movieId);
        $this->assertNotNull($episode, 'episode continue-watching row missing');
        $this->assertNotNull($movie, 'movie continue-watching row missing');

        // THE /app bug: top-level poster_url must be the resolved SERIES poster,
        // not the episode's stored TMDB still.
        $this->assertSame('/series/poster.jpg', $episode['poster_url']);
        $this->assertSame('/series/poster.jpg', $episode['metadata']['poster_url']);

        // Top-level runtime (minutes) drives the SPA progress bar; must be > 0.
        $this->assertIsInt($episode['runtime']);
        $this->assertGreaterThan(0, $episode['runtime']);
        $this->assertSame(42, $episode['runtime']);

        // id == media item id (not playback_state id); parent_id resolved from the
        // now-selected mi.parent_id column.
        $this->assertSame($this->episodeId, $episode['id']);
        $this->assertSame($this->episodeId, $episode['media_item_id']);
        $this->assertSame($this->seasonId, $episode['parent_id']);

        // Retained playback fields (raw ticks for useResumeSync) + metadata block.
        $this->assertSame(1000, $episode['position_ticks']);
        $this->assertSame(100000, $episode['duration_ticks']);
        $this->assertIsArray($episode['metadata']);

        // Movie keeps its own poster — series/season resolution never touches it.
        $this->assertSame('/movies/big.jpg', $movie['poster_url']);
        $this->assertSame($this->movieId, $movie['id']);
        $this->assertSame(120, $movie['runtime']);
    }

    private function seedFixtures(): void
    {
        $this->assertNotNull($this->db);

        $this->db->query(
            "INSERT INTO libraries (id, name, type, paths) VALUES (?, ?, 'series', '[]')",
            [$this->libraryId, 'CW IT Library'],
        );
        $this->db->query(
            "INSERT INTO users (id, username, email, password_hash)
             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
            [
                $this->userId,
                'cw-it-' . substr($this->userId, 0, 8),
                'cw-it-' . substr($this->userId, 0, 8) . '@example.test',
                'x',
            ],
        );

        $this->insertMediaItem($this->seriesId, null, 'series', 'CW IT Series', [
            'poster_url' => '/series/poster.jpg',
        ]);
        $this->insertMediaItem($this->seasonId, $this->seriesId, 'season', 'Season 1', [
            'poster_url' => '/season/poster.jpg',
            'season' => 1,
        ]);
        // Episode's stored poster IS its TMDB still — the wrong image for the rail.
        $this->insertMediaItem($this->episodeId, $this->seasonId, 'episode', 'S01E01', [
            'poster_url' => '/stills/ep01.jpg',
            'still_url' => '/stills/ep01.jpg',
            'season' => 1,
            'episode' => 1,
            'runtime' => 42,
        ]);
        $this->insertMediaItem($this->movieId, null, 'movie', 'Big Movie', [
            'poster_url' => '/movies/big.jpg',
            'runtime' => 120,
        ]);

        $this->db->query(
            "INSERT INTO sessions (id, user_id, device_id) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
            [$this->sessionId, $this->userId, 'cw-it-device'],
        );

        // In-progress playback (< 95%) for both the episode and the movie.
        $this->insertPlayback($this->episodeId, 1000, 100000);
        $this->insertPlayback($this->movieId, 5000, 100000);
    }

    /**
     * @param array<string, mixed> $metadata
     */
    private function insertMediaItem(string $id, ?string $parentId, string $type, string $name, array $metadata): void
    {
        $this->assertNotNull($this->db);
        $this->db->query(
            "INSERT INTO media_items (id, library_id, parent_id, name, type, path, metadata_json)
             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
            [
                $id,
                $this->libraryId,
                $parentId,
                $name,
                $type,
                '/cw-it/' . $id . '.mkv',
                (string) json_encode($metadata),
            ],
        );
    }

    private function insertPlayback(string $mediaItemId, int $position, int $duration): void
    {
        $this->assertNotNull($this->db);
        $this->db->query(
            "INSERT INTO playback_state
                (id, session_id, media_item_id, position_ticks, duration_ticks, playback_status)
             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'playing')",
            [Uuid::v4(), $this->sessionId, $mediaItemId, $position, $duration],
        );
    }

    /**
     * @param list<array<string, mixed>> $rows
     * @return array<string, mixed>|null
     */
    private function rowFor(array $rows, string $mediaItemId): ?array
    {
        foreach ($rows as $row) {
            if (($row['media_item_id'] ?? null) === $mediaItemId) {
                return $row;
            }
        }
        return null;
    }

    private function purgeFixtures(): void
    {
        if ($this->db === null) {
            return;
        }
        // Child-first (FKs cascade, but be explicit and id-scoped so a shared test
        // DB is left untouched apart from these rows).
        $this->db->query('DELETE FROM playback_state WHERE session_id = ?', [$this->sessionId]);
        $this->db->query('DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = ?', [$this->sessionId]);
        foreach ([$this->episodeId, $this->seasonId, $this->seriesId, $this->movieId] as $id) {
            if ($id !== '') {
                $this->db->query('DELETE FROM media_items WHERE id = ?', [$id]);
            }
        }
        if ($this->userId !== '') {
            $this->db->query('DELETE FROM users WHERE id = ?', [$this->userId]);
        }
        if ($this->libraryId !== '') {
            $this->db->query('DELETE FROM libraries WHERE id = ?', [$this->libraryId]);
        }
    }

    private function isMysqlReachable(string $host, int $port): bool
    {
        $sock = @fsockopen($host, $port, $errno, $errstr, 1.0);
        if ($sock === false) {
            return false;
        }
        fclose($sock);

        return true;
    }
}

     * @param array<string, mixed> $metadata
     */
    private function insertMediaItem(string $id, ?string $parentId, string $type, string $name, array $metadata): void
    {
        $this->assertNotNull($this->db);
        $this->db->query(
            "INSERT INTO media_items (id, library_id, parent_id, name, type, path, metadata_json)
             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
            [
                $id,
                $this->libraryId,
                $parentId,
                $name,
                $type,
                '/cw-it/' . $id . '.mkv',
                (string) json_encode($metadata),
            ],
        );
    }

    private function insertPlayback(string $mediaItemId, int $position, int $duration): void
    {
        $this->assertNotNull($this->db);
        $this->db->query(
            "INSERT INTO playback_state
                (id, session_id, media_item_id, position_ticks, duration_ticks, playback_status)
             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'playing')",
            [Uuid::v4(), $this->sessionId, $mediaItemId, $position, $duration],
        );
    }

    /**
     * @param list<array<string, mixed>> $rows
     * @return array<string, mixed>|null
     */
    private function rowFor(array $rows, string $mediaItemId): ?array
    {
        foreach ($rows as $row) {
            if (($row['media_item_id'] ?? null) === $mediaItemId) {
                return $row;
            }
        }
        return null;
    }

    private function purgeFixtures(): void
    {
        if ($this->db === null) {
            return;
        }
        // Child-first (FKs cascade, but be explicit and id-scoped so a shared test
        // DB is left untouched apart from these rows).
        $this->db->query('DELETE FROM playback_state WHERE session_id = ?', [$this->sessionId]);
        $this->db->query('DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = ?', [$this->sessionId]);
        foreach ([$this->episodeId, $this->seasonId, $this->seriesId, $this->movieId] as $id) {
            if ($id !== '') {
                $this->db->query('DELETE FROM media_items WHERE id = ?', [$id]);
            }
        }
        if ($this->userId !== '') {
            $this->db->query('DELETE FROM users WHERE id = ?', [$this->userId]);
        }
        if ($this->libraryId !== '') {
            $this->db->query('DELETE FROM libraries WHERE id = ?', [$this->libraryId]);
        }
    }

    private function isMysqlReachable(string $host, int $port): bool
    {
        $sock = @fsockopen($host, $port, $errno, $errstr, 1.0);
        if ($sock === false) {
            return false;
        }
        fclose($sock);

        return true;
    }
}

READ-ONLY review. Do NOT Edit/Write/commit/mutate anything — only read and report. Do NOT run `caliber refresh`. Always `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && <cmd>` in the same command.

Review the WS-A fix commit `b726f5c3` in /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server. Diff: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && git show b726f5c3 --stat && git show b726f5c3`. Files: `src/Session/PlaybackController.php`, `tests/Unit/Session/PlaybackControllerTest.php`, `tests/Integration/Session/ContinueWatchingIntegrationTest.php`.

Context: WS-A of /home/sites/phlix/plan_posters.md. getContinueWatching() must return MediaItemShaper-shaped rows so the /app SPA MediaCard gets top-level poster_url (series poster for episodes), top-level runtime (minutes), top-level id (= media item id), while retaining position_ticks/duration_ticks/media_item_id/metadata for the console + the rating gate filter (which keys on media_item_id).

Review for:
1. CORRECTNESS of the poster-resolution change. The commit REMOVED the prior "hasRealOwnPoster/still_url" heuristic so episodes now ALWAYS prefer series→season poster, only falling back to the episode's own poster when neither exists. The plan's stated ordering (A2) was "episode's own poster only if it's a genuine poster → season → parent series". Assess: is always-preferring-series a defensible interpretation (plan prose also says "the episode poster_url is currently the still, so prefer the series poster"), or a regression that loses genuinely-distinct episode art in the CW rail? Is there any case where an episode ends up with NO poster (blank card)? Verify the movie path is untouched (movies keep own poster).
2. Did adding `mi.parent_id` to inner+outer SELECT break the duplicate-column / `mi.id AS id` resolution or the ROW_NUMBER dedup? Check the SQL still parses correctly (no ambiguous columns) and the mapper reads parent_id correctly.
3. Does shaping still preserve media_item_id + metadata so `RatingGate::filterItems($items,$filter,'media_item_id')` (WebPortalRouter.php ~:1626-1645) keeps working? Confirm no regression to the gate.
4. Are the strengthened unit tests actually asserting the TOP-LEVEL poster_url and TOP-LEVEL runtime (the real /app bug), not just metadata.*? Is the changed test (own-poster→series-poster) a legitimate expectation change or does it hide a regression?
5. Integration test quality: does it follow the existing real-MySQL harness/skip convention correctly (compare to tests/Integration/Stats/MetricsReadQueriesTest.php)? Will it actually exercise the two-JOIN + dedup against a live DB (not a mock)? Are its assertions meaningful (episode→series poster, runtime, id, parent_id, retained ticks; movie keeps own)?
6. Any security/async/Webman-convention issues introduced.

Run to confirm current state (read-only):
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/Session/PlaybackController.php 2>&1 | tail -15`
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpunit --filter ContinueWatching 2>&1 | tail -20`

Report a NUMBERED findings list (severity blocker/major/minor, file:line, description, suggested fix) OR the literal `NO FINDINGS` if the change is fully correct and complete. Be decisive about whether the always-prefer-series behavior should stand or be reverted to the plan's own-if-genuine ordering.

  `home/index.tpl` + `partials/media_card.tpl`) has **zero callers** — it is dead code.
- phlix-hub: `src/Applic

## general-purpose — investigation — 2026-07-18

Read-only investigation. No code changed. See structured report returned to coordinator.

### Data path
- Route (server-rendered home): `PageRenderer::renderHome()` → `PlaybackController::getContinueWatching($userId, 10)` → assigns `continue_watching` → `home/index.tpl` foreach → `partials/media_card.tpl`.
  - `src/Server/WebPortal/PageRenderer.php:290-300`
  - `public/templates/home/index.tpl:12-21`
  - `public/templates/partials/media_card.tpl:1-25`
- JSON API (used by console/native clients): `GET /api/v1/users/me/continue-watching` → `WebPortalRouter::getContinueWatching()` (`src/Server/WebPortal/WebPortalRouter.php:1565-1584`) and `GET /api/v1/me/continue-watching` → `SessionController::getContinueWatching()` (`src/Server/Http/Controllers/SessionController.php:321-328`). Both call the same `PlaybackController::getContinueWatching`.

### CW SQL + shaper — `src/Session/PlaybackController.php:348-387`
Selects `ps.id, session_id, media_item_id, position_ticks, duration_ticks, playback_status, updated_at` + `mi.name, mi.type, mi.metadata_json`. Mapper adds `metadata = json_decode(metadata_json)`. NO `user_data` wrapper; NO `resume_position_ticks`; progress lives in TOP-LEVEL `position_ticks`/`duration_ticks`.

### Normal listing shaper — `ItemRepository::hydrateItem()` (`src/Media/Library/ItemRepository.php:2207-2216`)
`SELECT *` from media_items → `metadata = json_decode(metadata_json)`. IDENTICAL metadata decode to CW.

### Root causes
1. PROGRESS BAR (definitive, code bug): template `media_card.tpl:12` guards on `$item.user_data.resume_position_ticks > 0` and line 14 divides by `$item.metadata.runtime_ticks`. CW rows have NEITHER key — they expose flat `position_ticks`/`duration_ticks` and metadata carries `runtime` (mi

{"stdout":"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id CHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    display_name VARCHAR(255),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    last_login TIMESTAMP NULL,
    INDEX idx_username (username),
    INDEX idx_email (email)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

-- User settings table
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_settings (
    user_id CHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
    max_streams INT DEFAULT 3,
    max_bitrate INT DEFAULT 100000000,
    preferred_audio_language VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'en',
    preferred_subtitle_language VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'en',
    subtitle_mode ENUM('always', 'only_foreign', 'none') DEFAULT 'only_foreign',
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

-- Libraries table
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS libraries (
    id CHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    type ENUM('movie', 'series', 'music', 'photo', 'video') NOT NULL,
    paths JSON NOT NULL,
    options JSON,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    INDEX idx_type (type)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

-- Media items table

declare(strict_types=1);

use Phlix\Auth\UserProfileManager;
use Phlix\Auth\UserRepository;

/**
 * The single parental-control ACCESS gate shared by every user-facing read and
 * stream path, so the ~15 handlers that must honour a profile's content-rating
 * cap never re-implement (and never drift on) the effective-rating + allow-list
 * decision.
 *
 * It composes two concerns:
 *
 *  1. {@see resolveFilterForUser()} — turn a request's account id into the
 *     active profile's cap `['allowedRatings' => string[], 'allowUnrated' =>
 *     bool]`, or `null` (the permissive default: "no gate"). Null is returned
 *     for the account OWNER/admin, an unauthenticated request, no active
 *     profile, no cap configured, or the most-permissive cap — mirroring
 *     {@see \Phlix\Server\WebPortal\WebPortalRouter}'s existing browse logic so

## Reviewer (per-step) — WS-A commit `b726f5c3` — findings

**1 finding (minor). Verdict: the always-prefer-series behavior should STAND.**

### Decisiveness on the core question (always-prefer-series vs. "own-if-genuine")
The removal of the `hasRealOwnPoster`/`still_url` heuristic is **correct and should not be reverted.**
- It matches the **A2 acceptance criterion** verbatim: *"every episode CW row has `metadata.poster_url` = a real series poster when available."* The "own poster only if genuine → season → series" wording in the plan prose is self-contradicted by its own parenthetical ("prefer the series poster") and by the acceptance bullet; the acceptance bullet governs.
- It matches the golden contract (`MediaCard.vue` shows top-level `poster_url`; for episodes that must be the series art in a series-of-cards CW rail).
- The old heuristic was genuinely unsound: for non-TMDB episodes there is no `still_url` to compare against, so a still masqueraded as a "real own poster" and leaked a frame-grab into the rail. There is no reliable signal to distinguish a genuine episode poster from a still, so preferring the resolved series→season poster is the defensible interpretation.
- **No new blank-card case:** the episode's own `poster_url` is only overwritten when a series or season poster actually exists (non-nu

Small fix in repo /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server. Always `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && <cmd>` in the same command. Do NOT run `caliber refresh`. No PRs. Commit directly to master. phpstan config `phpstan.neon`; phpcs PSR12.

A review of the WS-A integration test found ONE minor gap. Fix ONLY this:

File `tests/Integration/Session/ContinueWatchingIntegrationTest.php`: the class docblock claims the test exercises the `ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ps.media_item_id …)` dedup, but `seedFixtures()` inserts exactly ONE `playback_state` row per media item, so the PARTITION collapses 1→1 and the dedup path is never actually proven.

Fix: In `seedFixtures()` insert a SECOND `playback_state` row for the SAME episode media item (`$this->episodeId`) under a second session (or same session with a distinct id) with an OLDER `updated_at` (and different position_ticks so you can tell them apart). Then in the assertion, assert the result contains EXACTLY ONE row for that episode (dedup collapsed the two to one) AND that it is the NEWER one (the one with the more recent `updated_at` / its position_ticks). This genuinely exercises `PARTITION BY ps.media_item_id ORDER BY ps.updated_at DESC, ps.id DESC`. Make sure the id-scoped teardown also removes the second playback_state row (add its id to the purge list). Keep the docblock claim accurate (it can now legitimately claim dedup coverage).

Read the current test file first to match its exact fixture/session/teardown patterns and column names. Do not change production code. Do not touch any other test.

VERIFY:
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpstan analyse tests/Integration/Session/ContinueWatchingIntegrationTest.php 2>&1 | tail -15` (note: tests may not be in phpstan paths — run it explicitly anyway and report)
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpunit tests/Integration/Session/ContinueWatchingIntegrationTest.php 2>&1 | tail -20` (it will self-skip without a DB — report whether it skipped or ran; that's expected).
- Also run `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpunit --filter ContinueWatching 2>&1 | tail -20` to confirm no unit regressions.

COMMIT: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && git add -A && git commit -m "test(ws-a): seed second playback_state row to genuinely exercise ROW_NUMBER dedup in CW integration test" && git push origin master`. Report: exact changes (file:line), verification output, commit hash. Report is DATA for the parent.

READ-ONLY review. Do NOT Edit/Write/commit/mutate anything — only read and report. Do NOT run `caliber refresh`. Always `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-console-client && <cmd>` in the same command.

Review WS-B fix commit `4461e28` in /home/sites/phlix/phlix-console-client. Diff: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-console-client && git show 4461e28 --stat && git show 4461e28`.

Context: WS-B of /home/sites/phlix/plan_posters.md. Console poster rails/detail/search must resolve RELATIVE signed poster URLs (e.g. `/api/v1/artwork/{id}?…`) to absolute via a `baseUrl` + `resolveUrl()` BEFORE the scheme check (dropping non-http was the bug). This commit fixes a resolve-before-scheme-check bug in `src/Screen/PhotoAlbumScreen.php` + `src/Screen/PhotosScreen.php`, and repairs previously-skipped/vacuous tests.

Review for:
1. CORRECTNESS of the Photos fix: is `resolveUrl()` now applied BEFORE the scheme check in both PhotoAlbumScreen and PhotosScreen, with the RESOLVED url passed to the loader (not double-resolved, not dropped)? Confirm it mirrors the correct DetailScreen pattern (loadChildPostersIn ~:440-451). Any double-resolve or empty-url regression?
2. Are the two previously-RED Photos tests now genuinely passing for the RIGHT reason (real local server, relative→absolute resolution actually exercised), not vacuously?
3. SearchScreen tests: the 4 unskipped tests — do they now genuinely assert resolution (relative loads against a real local server; empty/malformed/non-http via deterministic scheduled-load count)? Or are they still weak?
4. DetailScreen + BrowseScreen rewritten tests: do they now assert a RESOLVED absolute load is scheduled (not the old false "no load" semantics)?
5. THE SEMANTIC CONCERN — the implementer flagged that `resolveUrl()` prefixes the base onto ANY non-`http(s)` string, so `ftp://x` → `https://srv/ftp://x` and a "malformed" scheme-less value → `https://srv/<value>`, both of which then PASS the scheme check and schedule a load (only the EMPTY string is skipped). Assess: (a) is this a real production risk given the server only ever emits relative signed paths or absolute https URLs? (b) Did the implementer correctly adapt the tests to assert TRUE behavior rather than hide a bug? (c) Should production reject genuinely-non-http ABSOLUTE urls while still resolving scheme-less relative paths, or is prefix-everything acceptable? Give a decisive recommendation.
6. Any security (SSRF/path traversal via crafted poster_url), correctness, or SugarCraft/ReactPHP async issues.
7. Confirm MediaQueryTest now asserts topLevel==='1'.

Run (read-only):
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-console-client && vendor/bin/phpstan analyse 2>&1 | tail -12`
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-console-client && vendor/bin/phpunit tests/Screen/PhotoAlbumScreenTest.php tests/Screen/PhotosScreenTest.php tests/Screen/SearchScreenTest.php tests/Screen/DetailScreenTest.php tests/Screen/BrowseScreenTest.php tests/Api/MediaQueryTest.php 2>&1 | tail -15`

Report a NUMBERED findings list (severity, file:line, description, suggested fix) OR literal `NO FINDINGS`. Be decisive on the resolveUrl non-http concern (#5): is it a blocker/major/minor, or acceptable-as-is? Note: 12 PlayerScreenTest failures are known pre-existing/out-of-scope — ignore them.

{"stdout":"- `src/App.php:957-962` (`BrowseScreen` wired **with** `baseUrl`), `:1174-1181` (`DetailScreen` **without**),
  `:1189-1194` (`SearchScreen` **without**).
- `src/Media/PosterLoader.php` — cache key is `(url, w, h, protocol)`; changing the URL re-keys naturally — **no
  `FORMAT_VERSION` bump needed**.

### Root causes

- **A) Anime/TV rails show stills:** `forLibrary()` omits `topLevel=1`, so `GET /api/v1/media` returns **episodes**
  (whose `poster_url` is the still). Movies are top-level, so they render posters.
- **C) Detail page shows no image (any type):** server serves `poster_url` as a **relative** signed path
  (`/api/v1/artwork/{id}?…`); `DetailScreen`/`SearchScreen` lack `baseUrl`+`resolveUrl()` and drop non-`http` URLs.
  Only `BrowseScreen` resolves them.

### Steps (each = one §2.2 cycle)

- **B1 — Investigate (read-only).** Confirm `forLibrary`/`toParams`; whether drilling *into* a series uses a
  `parentId` query (must NOT get `topLevel`); `DetailScreen`/`SearchScreen` ctor signatures + `App.php` call sites;
  the exact `resolveUrl()` in `BrowseScreen`.
- **B2 — Rails request top-level items (fix A).** Make the home library rails request `topLevel=1` (add a
  `topLevel` arg to `forLibrary()` and confirm `toParams()` emits `topLevel=1`, or set it at the call site in
  `fetchLibraryMedia`). **Guard:** do NOT apply `topLevel=1` when drilled into a series (`parentId` present) —
  that view must still list episodes. Confirm the drill-in path (LibraryScreen/OpenLibraryMsg) is unaffected.
  Acceptance: rails show posters; drilling into a series still lists episodes.
- **B3 — Detail images (fix C).** Add a `baseUrl` ctor param + private `resolveUrl()` to `DetailScreen` (mirror
  `BrowseScreen`'s), apply it before the scheme check in `fetchHero()` and `loadChildPostersIn()`; wire
  `$this->api->baseUrl()` at `App.php:1174-1181`. Acceptance: hero + child-episode thumbnails render.
- **B4 — Search images.** Same `baseUrl`+`res
